questions.stem: Under ASC 321, how should an equity investment without a readily determinable fair value be measured if the entity does not elect the fair value option?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At cost less impairment, plus or minus observable price changes (measurement alternative)

choices.label: B
choices.text: At lower of cost or market

choices.label: C
choices.text: Using the equity method

choices.label: D
choices.text: At fair value through OCI

questions.explanation: ASC 321-10-35-2 provides a measurement alternative for equity securities without readily determinable fair values. Under this alternative, the investment is measured at cost, minus impairment, plus or minus changes resulting from observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments of the same issuer.
questions.explanationJa: 容易に決定可能な公正価値のない持分証券には測定の代替(measurement alternative)がある。取得原価 - 減損 +/- 観察可能な価格変動（同一発行者の同一・類似投資の秩序ある取引に基づく）で測定する。

questions.stem: Investor Corp owns 30% of Investee Corp and uses the equity method. Investee reported net income of $200,000 and paid dividends of $50,000. What is the effect on Investor's income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Investment income of $60,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investment income of $45,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Dividend income of $15,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Investment income of $60,000 and dividend income of $15,000

questions.explanation: Under the equity method (ASC 323-10-35-4), the investor recognizes its share of the investee's net income: 30% × $200,000 = $60,000 as investment income. Dividends received ($50,000 × 30% = $15,000) reduce the investment account but are not recognized as income.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法では被投資会社の純利益に対する持分を認識する。投資収益 = 30% x $200,000 = $60,000。受取配当金 = $50,000 x 30% = $15,000は投資勘定の減額であり、収益ではない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 320, unrealized gains and losses on available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities are reported in:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Other comprehensive income (OCI)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings directly

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not recognized until realized

questions.explanation: ASC 320-10-35-1 requires AFS debt securities to be measured at fair value, with unrealized gains and losses excluded from earnings and reported in other comprehensive income (accumulated OCI on the balance sheet). They are reclassified to earnings when sold or impaired.
questions.explanationJa: AFS負債証券は公正価値で測定し、未実現損益は当期利益でなくOCI（その他の包括利益）に計上する。売却時または減損認識時にOCIから当期利益へ振り替える（リサイクリング）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 321, unrealized gains and losses on equity securities with readily determinable fair values are reported in:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Other comprehensive income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings directly

choices.label: D
choices.text: A separate component of equity

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-01 (codified in ASC 321-10-35-1) requires equity securities with readily determinable fair values to be measured at fair value with changes recognized in net income. This eliminated the previous available-for-sale category for equity securities.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-01により、公正価値が容易に決定可能な持分証券は公正価値で測定し、変動を当期純利益に認識する。従来の持分証券のAFS区分は廃止された。負債証券と異なりOCIへの計上は認められない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 326-30, how is impairment of an available-for-sale debt security recognized when the entity intends to sell the security?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Write down to fair value through OCI only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Write down the entire difference between amortized cost and fair value through earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize only the credit loss portion in earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: No impairment recognition is required until actual sale

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-4, if the entity intends to sell (or more likely than not will be required to sell) the AFS debt security before recovery, the entire difference between amortized cost and fair value is recognized in earnings. If the entity does not intend to sell, only the credit loss portion is recognized in earnings through an allowance.
questions.explanationJa: AFS負債証券の減損は売却意図の有無で処理が異なる。売却意図あり（または売却を余儀なくされる可能性が高い）: 償却原価とFVの差額全額を当期利益に認識。売却意図なし: 信用損失部分のみ引当金を通じて当期利益に認識し、残りはOCI。

questions.stem: Held-to-maturity debt securities are reported on the balance sheet at:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Amortized cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: Lower of cost or market

choices.label: D
choices.text: Face value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-25-1, held-to-maturity (HTM) securities are reported at amortized cost. The entity must have the positive intent and ability to hold the security to maturity. Any premium or discount is amortized using the effective interest method.
questions.explanationJa: HTM証券はB/S上で償却原価により報告する。分類要件として、満期まで保有する積極的な意図と能力（positive intent and ability）が必要。プレミアム・ディスカウントは実効金利法で償却する。

questions.stem: Investor A acquired 25% of Company B for $500,000. The book value of Company B's net assets is $1,600,000. The excess of cost over underlying book value is attributed to equipment (fair value exceeds book value by $200,000, 10-year remaining life). What is Investor A's equity method income if Company B reports net income of $300,000?
questions.explanation: Share of net income: 25% × $300,000 = $75,000. Excess cost allocated to equipment: 25% × $200,000 = $50,000, amortized over 10 years = $5,000/year. Equity method income: $75,000 - $5,000 = $70,000.

questions.stem: When a debt security is transferred from held-to-maturity to available-for-sale, the security is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recorded at amortized cost with no adjustment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recorded at fair value with unrealized gain/loss in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recorded at fair value with unrealized gain/loss in net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recorded at the lower of amortized cost or fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10, when a HTM security is transferred to AFS, the security is reclassified at fair value. The unrealized holding gain or loss at the date of transfer is reported in OCI.
questions.explanationJa: HTMからAFSへの振替では、証券を公正価値で再分類する。振替日時点の未実現保有損益はOCIに計上する（当期利益ではない）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 825 (Fair Value Option), an entity elects to measure a financial liability at fair value. Where are subsequent changes in fair value attributable to instrument-specific credit risk reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Other comprehensive income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 825-10-45-5, for financial liabilities measured under the fair value option, changes in fair value attributable to instrument-specific credit risk are presented separately in OCI. All other changes in fair value are recognized in net income.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値オプション(FVO)で測定する金融負債では、自社の信用リスク(instrument-specific credit risk)に起因するFV変動をOCIに計上する。その他のFV変動は当期純利益に認識する。これにより自社の信用悪化で利益が出る逆説的結果を回避する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 810, which of the following generally requires consolidation of an investee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ownership of 20% or more of voting interests

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ownership of more than 50% of voting interests (controlling financial interest)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Significant influence over the investee

choices.label: D
choices.text: Any equity investment in the investee

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15-8, a parent with a controlling financial interest (usually more than 50% of voting interests) must consolidate. 20-50% ownership typically uses the equity method; less than 20% uses fair value under ASC 321.
questions.explanationJa: 支配的持分（通常は議決権50%超）を有する親会社は連結が必要。20-50%の重要な影響力がある場合は持分法。20%未満はASC 321に基づき公正価値（またはmeasurement alternative）で測定。

questions.stem: Investor owns 30% of Investee. Investee reports net income of $600,000. During the year, Investee sold inventory to Investor for $200,000 (cost $140,000). At year-end, 50% of this inventory remains unsold by Investor. What is Investor's equity method income?
questions.explanation: Share of income: 30% × $600,000 = $180,000. Unrealized profit in ending inventory = ($200,000 - $140,000) × 50% = $30,000. Investor's share of unrealized profit: 30% × $30,000 = $9,000. Equity income = $180,000 - $9,000 = $171,000.

questions.stem: Entity R purchases a $100,000 face value bond at $95,000. The bond pays 5% annually and has 5 years to maturity. The effective interest rate is 6.19%. What is interest revenue in Year 1 using the effective interest method?
questions.explanation: Interest revenue = carrying amount × effective rate = $95,000 × 6.19% = $5,881 (rounded). Cash received = $100,000 × 5% = $5,000. Discount amortization = $5,881 - $5,000 = $881. New carrying value = $95,881.

questions.stem: Entity S purchased an AFS debt security for $50,000 (amortized cost). At December 31 Year 1, fair value is $53,000. In Year 2, the security is sold for $54,000. Ignoring amortization, what gain is recognized in Year 2 earnings?
questions.explanation: On sale, the realized gain is measured against amortized cost: $54,000 - $50,000 = $4,000 recognized in earnings. The $3,000 previously in OCI is reclassified to earnings as part of this $4,000 total gain. The additional $1,000 ($54,000 - $53,000) is the Year 2 increment.
questions.explanationJa: 売却時の実現利益は償却原価に対して測定：$54,000 - $50,000 = $4,000が当期利益に認識。OCIに計上されていた$3,000は当期利益に振り替えられる。$1,000（$54,000 - $53,000）はY2の追加増分。

questions.stem: Investor owns 300,000 shares (30%) of Investee (total 1,000,000 shares). Book value of Investee is $3,000,000. Investment carrying value is $900,000. Investee issues 250,000 new shares at $4/share to third parties (book value was $3/share). What gain or loss does Investor recognize on dilution?
questions.explanation: After issuance: Investee BV = $3,000,000 + (250,000 × $4) = $4,000,000. Investor now owns 300,000/1,250,000 = 24%. Investor's share of BV = 24% × $4,000,000 = $960,000. Carrying value was $900,000. Gain on dilution = $960,000 - $900,000 = $60,000.

questions.stem: Entity T purchases a $200,000 face HTM bond at $210,000. Stated rate is 8%, effective rate is 7%. What is interest revenue in Year 2?

Investor carries an equity method investment at $480,000. Due to sustained losses and adverse industry conditions, fair value declines to $420,000 and the decline is deemed other-than-temporary. What is the impairment loss?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-32, if a decline in value of an equity method investment is other-than-temporary, the investment is written down to fair value. Impairment loss = $480,000 - $420,000 = $60,000, recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法投資の価値下落がother-than-temporary（一時的でない）場合、公正価値まで評価減する。減損損失 = $480,000 - $420,000 = $60,000。当期利益に認識し、新たな原価基礎となる（回復しても戻入不可）。

questions.stem: Entity U holds a trading securities portfolio. Cost: $300,000. Fair value at Dec 31 Year 1: $285,000. Fair value at Dec 31 Year 2: $310,000. What unrealized gain or loss is recognized in Year 2 net income?
questions.explanation: Trading securities are marked to fair value each period with changes in net income. Year 2 change = $310,000 - $285,000 = $25,000 unrealized gain. The Year 1 loss of $15,000 was already recognized in Year 1. Each year's income reflects only that year's FV change.
questions.explanationJa: 売買目的有価証券は毎期公正価値で評価し変動を純利益に認識。Y2の変動 = $310,000 - $285,000 = $25,000の未実現利益。Y1の$15,000損失はY1に既に認識済み。各年度はその年の公正価値変動のみを反映。

questions.stem: Entity V owned 10% of Investee at a cost of $80,000 (FV method). The 10% stake's fair value is now $120,000. Entity V acquires an additional 20% for $240,000, giving it 30% and significant influence. What gain is recognized on the previously held interest at the transition to equity method?
questions.explanation: When transitioning to equity method, the previously held interest is remeasured to fair value. Gain = FV $120,000 - carrying value $80,000 = $40,000. The equity method investment begins at $120,000 + $240,000 = $360,000.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法への移行時、既保有持分を公正価値で再測定する。利益 = FV $120,000 - 帳簿価額$80,000 = $40,000。持分法投資の開始簿価 = 既保有分FV $120,000 + 追加取得$240,000 = $360,000。

questions.stem: Investor holds 40% of Investee using the equity method. Investee reports net income of $300,000 and OCI of $50,000 (foreign currency translation). No dividends were paid. What is the total increase in Investor's investment account?
questions.explanation: Investment account increases by investor's share of both net income and OCI: Income portion: 40% × $300,000 = $120,000 (recognized in investor's NI). OCI portion: 40% × $50,000 = $20,000 (recognized in investor's OCI). Total increase = $120,000 + $20,000 = $140,000.
questions.explanationJa: 投資勘定は純利益とOCI両方の持分で増加：利益部分：40% × $300,000 = $120,000（投資者のNIに認識）。OCI部分：40% × $50,000 = $20,000（投資者のOCIに認識）。合計増加 = $120,000 + $20,000 = $140,000。

questions.stem: Entity W holds a HTM bond at amortized cost of $100,000. Fair value is $92,000. Management estimates expected credit losses of $5,000. What is the net carrying value of the bond after recording the credit loss allowance?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20, HTM securities are measured at amortized cost less an allowance for expected credit losses. Allowance = $5,000. Net carrying value = $100,000 - $5,000 = $95,000. The remaining decline ($8,000 - $5,000 = $3,000) is attributable to market factors, not credit, and is not recognized for HTM.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20に基づき、HTM証券は償却原価から信用損失引当金を控除して測定する。引当金 = $5,000。正味帳簿価額 = $100,000 - $5,000 = $95,000。残りの下落$3,000は信用以外の市場要因によるもので、HTMでは認識しない。

questions.stem: Entity A invests $2,000,000 in a private equity fund that reports net asset value (NAV) per share. The fund has no unfunded commitments and a redemption period of 90 days. Under ASC 820, can Entity A use NAV as a practical expedient for fair value measurement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, if the investment meets the criteria for the NAV practical expedient

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, all investments must use Level 1, 2, or 3 inputs

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only if the fund is publicly traded

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because private equity funds are always Level 3

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-35-59, entities may use NAV per share as a practical expedient to estimate the fair value of certain investments in funds (including private equity, hedge funds, and real estate funds) that calculate NAV per share consistent with ASC 946. Investments measured using the NAV practical expedient are not categorized within the fair value hierarchy levels.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-35-59により、ASC 946に準拠してNAVを算出するファンド（プライベートエクイティ、ヘッジファンド、不動産ファンド等）への投資の公正価値を見積もるため、1株当たりNAVを実務上の便法として使用できる。NAV便法で測定された投資は公正価値ヒエラルキーのレベルに分類されない。

questions.subtopic: Equity Securities Without Readily Determinable FV
questions.stem: Entity B acquires equity securities of a private company for $500,000. The securities do not have a readily determinable fair value. Entity B does not elect the fair value option. Under ASC 321, how should Entity B measure these securities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At cost, with no subsequent adjustments

choices.label: B
choices.text: At cost minus impairment, plus or minus changes resulting from observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments

choices.label: C
choices.text: At fair value through net income using Level 3 inputs

choices.label: D
choices.text: At lower of cost or market

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-2, equity securities without readily determinable fair values may use the measurement alternative: cost minus impairment, plus or minus changes resulting from observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments of the same issuer. This is an election made on an instrument-by-instrument basis.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-2により、容易に決定可能な公正価値のない株式証券は測定代替法を使用できる：取得原価 − 減損 ± 同一発行者の同一または類似投資に関する秩序ある取引における観察可能な価格変動。この選択は金融商品ごとに行う。

questions.stem: Entity C purchases available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities for $200,000. At year-end, the fair value is $215,000. There is no credit loss. What journal entry should Entity C record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Investment $15,000; Credit Unrealized Gain - Net Income $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Investment $15,000; Credit Unrealized Gain - OCI $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No entry; AFS securities are held at cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Investment $15,000; Credit Revenue $15,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-1, AFS debt securities are reported at fair value with unrealized gains and losses (excluding credit losses) reported in other comprehensive income (OCI). The $15,000 unrealized gain ($215,000 − $200,000) is recorded as a credit to OCI, not net income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-1により、AFS負債証券は公正価値で報告し、未実現損益（信用損失を除く）はその他の包括利益（OCI）で報告する。未実現利益$15,000（$215,000 − $200,000）はOCIの貸方に計上し、純利益ではない。

questions.subtopic: Trading Securities - Unrealized vs Realized Gain
questions.stem: Entity D purchases trading securities for $80,000 on March 1. At December 31, the fair value is $95,000. On February 15 of the next year, Entity D sells the securities for $100,000. What gain does Entity D recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Trading securities are marked to fair value through net income. In Year 1, Entity D recognized $15,000 unrealized gain ($95,000 − $80,000) in net income. The carrying value at January 1, Year 2 is $95,000. Upon sale in Year 2, gain = $100,000 − $95,000 = $5,000 recognized in net income.
questions.explanationJa: 売買目的有価証券は公正価値で測定し変動を純利益に認識する。Year 1に未実現利益$15,000（$95,000 − $80,000）を純利益で認識。Year 2期首の帳簿価額は$95,000。Year 2の売却時の利益 = $100,000 − $95,000 = $5,000を純利益で認識。

questions.stem: Investor E owns 40% of Investee F. Investee F sells inventory to Investor E for $500,000 (cost to F: $300,000). At year-end, 30% of the inventory remains unsold by Investor E. What is the intercompany profit that Investor E must eliminate from its equity method income?
questions.explanation: Intercompany profit = $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000. Unsold portion = 30% × $200,000 = $60,000 unrealized profit remaining in inventory. Investor E's share = 40% × $60,000 = $24,000. This $24,000 is eliminated from the investor's share of investee income under the equity method.
questions.explanationJa: グループ間利益 = $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000。未販売部分 = 30% × $200,000 = $60,000の未実現利益が棚卸資産に残存。Investor Eの持分 = 40% × $60,000 = $24,000。この$24,000は持分法による投資先利益から消去する。

questions.stem: Entity G purchases a 5-year, $1,000,000 face value bond classified as HTM at $950,000 (discount of $50,000). The stated rate is 6% and the effective rate is 7.1%. What is the interest income in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method (ASC 835-30-35-2), interest income = Carrying amount × Effective rate. Year 1 interest income = $950,000 × 7.1% = $67,450. Cash received = $1,000,000 × 6% = $60,000. Discount amortization = $67,450 − $60,000 = $7,450. New carrying amount = $950,000 + $7,450 = $957,450.

questions.subtopic: Debt Security Reclassification - AFS to HTM
questions.stem: Entity H reclassifies a debt security from AFS to HTM. At the reclassification date, the amortized cost is $100,000, the fair value is $105,000, and there is $5,000 of unrealized gain in AOCI. How is the unrealized gain treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized immediately in net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remains in AOCI and amortized over the remaining life as a yield adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Eliminated against retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transferred to a valuation allowance

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10(c), when AFS securities are reclassified to HTM, the security is transferred at fair value ($105,000), which becomes the new amortized cost basis. The unrealized gain ($5,000) in AOCI is not immediately recognized in income but is amortized over the remaining life of the security as a yield adjustment, similar to a premium.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-10(c)により、AFS証券をHTMに再分類する場合、公正価値($105,000)で移管し、それが新たな償却原価基準となる。AOCIの未実現利益($5,000)は即時に純利益で認識せず、証券の残存期間にわたりプレミアムと同様に利回り調整として償却する。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Investee Loss Exceeding Investment
questions.stem: Investor J owns 30% of Investee K. Investor J's equity method investment balance is $100,000. Investee K reports a loss of $500,000 for the year. Investor J has no other investments in or commitments to Investee K. What should Investor J recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $150,000 (full 30% share)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $100,000 (reduce investment to zero)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $50,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No loss; the investment is already at zero

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-19, if the investor's share of losses ($500,000 × 30% = $150,000) exceeds the carrying amount of the investment ($100,000), the investor recognizes losses only up to the carrying amount, reducing the investment to zero. The investor discontinues applying the equity method. The remaining $50,000 is not recognized unless the investor has guaranteed obligations or is otherwise committed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-19により、投資先損失の持分（$500,000 × 30% = $150,000）が投資の帳簿価額($100,000)を超える場合、投資を零にするまでの損失のみ認識する。残りの$50,000は投資者が債務保証等のコミットメントがない限り認識しない。持分法の適用を中止する。

questions.stem: Entity L holds a corporate bond that is not actively traded. The fair value is estimated using observable market data for similar bonds, adjusted for differences in credit quality and maturity. Under ASC 820, what level in the fair value hierarchy would this bond be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Level 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: Level 2

choices.label: C
choices.text: Level 3

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not categorized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-35-47, Level 2 inputs include quoted prices for similar assets in active markets or quoted prices for identical assets in markets that are not active. Observable inputs adjusted for differences (like credit quality and maturity) also fall within Level 2. Level 1 requires quoted prices for identical assets in active markets. Level 3 uses unobservable inputs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-35-47により、レベル2のインプットには活発な市場での類似資産の相場価格や活発でない市場での同一資産の相場価格が含まれる。差異（信用度、満期等）について調整された観察可能なインプットもレベル2に該当する。レベル1は活発な市場での同一資産の相場価格、レベル3は観察不能なインプットを使用する。

questions.stem: Entity M holds equity securities of a private company measured using the measurement alternative (cost minus impairment ± observable price changes). In Year 2, Entity M observes an orderly transaction for similar securities of the same issuer at $30 per share. Entity M's cost was $25 per share for 10,000 shares. What adjustment should Entity M record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase investment by $50,000 with a gain in net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase investment by $50,000 with a credit to OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment; the measurement alternative does not allow upward adjustments

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase investment by $50,000 with a credit to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-2, the measurement alternative allows both upward and downward adjustments based on observable price changes. Entity M increases the investment by ($30 − $25) × 10,000 = $50,000 and recognizes a gain of $50,000 in net income. Observable price changes are recognized through earnings, not OCI.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-2により、測定代替法は観察可能な価格変動に基づく上方・下方の両方の調整を認める。Entity Mは投資を($30 − $25) × 10,000 = $50,000増額し、$50,000の利得を純利益で認識する。観察可能な価格変動はOCIではなく損益を通じて認識する。

questions.stem: Entity N purchases a $500,000 face value AFS debt security at $470,000 (discount of $30,000). The security has 3 years to maturity and the effective interest rate is 8.5%. After Year 1, what is the amortized cost?
questions.explanation: Interest income = $470,000 × 8.5% = $39,950. Cash received (assuming annual coupon at stated rate that yields $30,000 discount over 3 years) needs to be determined. With effective rate 8.5%: Interest income = $39,950. Stated coupon = approximately $500,000 × 6% = $30,000. Discount amortization = $39,950 − $30,000 = $9,950. Amortized cost = $470,000 + $9,950 = $479,950.

questions.stem: Investor P initially owns 10% of Investee Q (reported at FV through NI). Investor P acquires an additional 20%, bringing ownership to 30%. The equity method now applies. How should the transition be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Apply the equity method retroactively from the original investment date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the equity method prospectively from the date significant influence is obtained; adjust the existing investment to the new basis

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue reporting the entire investment at fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Write off the original 10% investment and start fresh

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-33, when an investor increases its ownership to a level requiring the equity method, the equity method is applied prospectively from the date significant influence is obtained. The carrying amount of the previously held investment becomes part of the equity method investment basis. Any difference between the new cost and the underlying equity is treated as basis differences.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-33により、投資者が持分法が必要な水準まで所有率を増加させた場合、重要な影響力を獲得した日から将来に向けて持分法を適用する。従前保有していた投資の帳簿価額が持分法投資の基礎の一部となる。新たな取得原価と基礎的持分の差額は基礎差額として処理する。

questions.stem: Entity R owns 45% of Entity S's voting stock. Entity S has no other significant shareholders, and Entity R does not control Entity S's board. Under ASC 810, should Entity R consolidate Entity S?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because 45% ownership represents a controlling interest

choices.label: B
choices.text: No; Entity R should use the equity method because it has significant influence but not control

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, because ownership exceeds 20%

choices.label: D
choices.text: No; Entity R should report the investment at fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15-8, consolidation is required when a parent has a controlling financial interest, generally through ownership of more than 50% of outstanding voting shares (for a voting interest entity). With 45% and no board control, Entity R has significant influence but not control. The equity method (ASC 323) applies for investments of 20-50% with significant influence.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-15-8により、支配的財務持分を有する場合（一般に議決権の50%超の所有）に連結が必要。45%で取締役会の支配がない場合、Entity Rは重要な影響力を有するが支配はしていない。20-50%で重要な影響力がある投資には持分法（ASC 323）が適用される。

questions.stem: Entity T holds trading equity securities with a fair value of $300,000 at the beginning of the year. During the year, Entity T receives cash dividends of $12,000. At year-end, the fair value is $320,000. What total amount should Entity T recognize in net income related to these securities?
questions.explanation: For trading securities, both dividend income and unrealized gains/losses are recognized in net income. Dividend income = $12,000. Unrealized gain = $320,000 − $300,000 = $20,000. Total in net income = $12,000 + $20,000 = $32,000. Dividends and fair value changes are presented separately or together depending on the entity's policy.
questions.explanationJa: 売買目的有価証券では、配当収入と未実現損益の両方が純利益で認識される。配当収入 = $12,000。未実現利益 = $320,000 − $300,000 = $20,000。純利益の合計 = $12,000 + $20,000 = $32,000。配当と公正価値変動は企業の方針により別個にまたは合算して表示する。

questions.subtopic: AFS Credit Loss vs Non-Credit Loss
questions.stem: Entity U holds an AFS debt security with amortized cost of $200,000 and fair value of $170,000 (decline of $30,000). Entity U determines that $10,000 of the decline is due to credit deterioration and $20,000 is due to changes in interest rates. How should Entity U record this?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit loss of $10,000 through an allowance (charged to income); non-credit loss of $20,000 in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: Total impairment of $30,000 charged to income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total decline of $30,000 recorded in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: No recognition until the security is sold

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-3, for AFS debt securities, credit losses are recognized through an allowance for credit losses (charged to credit loss expense in income), limited to the amount that fair value is below amortized cost. The non-credit portion of the decline ($20,000) is recognized in OCI. Allowance = $10,000; OCI loss = $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30-35-3により、AFS負債証券の信用損失は信用損失引当金（損益での信用損失費用）を通じて認識し、公正価値が償却原価を下回る金額を上限とする。下落の非信用部分($20,000)はOCIで認識する。引当金 = $10,000、OCI損失 = $20,000。

questions.stem: Entity V and Entity W each contribute $1,000,000 to form a 50/50 joint venture (JV). The JV is a separate legal entity and neither party has unilateral control. How should Entity V account for its investment in the JV?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Consolidate the JV proportionately

choices.label: B
choices.text: Account for the investment using the equity method

choices.label: C
choices.text: Report the investment at fair value through net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Account for the investment at cost

questions.explanation: Under US GAAP (ASC 323-10-15-4), corporate joint ventures in which the investor has significant influence (typically 20-50% ownership) are accounted for using the equity method. Proportionate consolidation is not permitted under US GAAP (though it is allowed under IFRS in limited circumstances). Neither party controls the JV, so full consolidation is not appropriate.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAP（ASC 323-10-15-4）では、投資者が重要な影響力を有する共同事業体（通常20-50%の所有）は持分法で会計処理する。US GAAPでは比例連結は認められない（IFRSでは限定的に許容）。いずれの当事者もJVを支配していないため、完全連結は適切でない。

questions.stem: Investor X paid $2,000,000 for a 25% interest in Investee Y. Investee Y's identifiable net assets at acquisition had a fair value of $6,000,000. The implied goodwill in the equity method investment is being reviewed. What is the goodwill embedded in Investor X's investment?
questions.explanation: Investor X's share of identifiable net assets = 25% × $6,000,000 = $1,500,000. Cost of investment = $2,000,000. Goodwill embedded in the investment = $2,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $500,000. Under the equity method, goodwill is not separately tested for impairment; instead, the entire investment is tested under ASC 323-10-35-32.
questions.explanationJa: Investor Xの識別可能純資産の持分 = 25% × $6,000,000 = $1,500,000。投資の取得原価 = $2,000,000。投資に含まれるのれん = $2,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $500,000。持分法では、のれんは個別に減損テストせず、投資全体をASC 323-10-35-32に基づきテストする。

questions.subtopic: Debt Security Interest Income at Acquisition
questions.stem: Entity Z purchases a bond between interest dates on April 1 for $1,030,000, which includes $30,000 of accrued interest. The face value is $1,000,000. What is the cost basis of the bond?
questions.explanation: When purchasing a bond between interest dates, the buyer pays the seller for accrued interest since the last interest payment date. The accrued interest ($30,000) is recorded as interest receivable, not as part of the bond's cost. The cost basis of the bond = $1,030,000 − $30,000 = $1,000,000 (at par in this case).
questions.explanationJa: 利払日の間に社債を購入する場合、買い手は前回の利払日からの経過利息を売り手に支払う。経過利息($30,000)は未収利息として計上し、社債の取得原価には含めない。社債の取得原価 = $1,030,000 − $30,000 = $1,000,000（この場合は額面価額）。

questions.stem: Entity A reclassifies a debt security from AFS to trading. At the reclassification date, the amortized cost is $90,000 and the fair value is $95,000. There is $5,000 of unrealized gain in AOCI. How is the transfer accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The $5,000 unrealized gain in AOCI is reclassified to net income immediately

choices.label: B
choices.text: The $5,000 remains in AOCI and is amortized

choices.label: C
choices.text: The security is transferred at amortized cost; no gain recognized

choices.label: D
choices.text: The $5,000 is recorded as a direct adjustment to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10(b), when AFS securities are transferred to trading, the unrealized gain or loss in AOCI at the date of transfer is immediately reclassified and recognized in net income. The security is carried at fair value ($95,000) in the trading portfolio going forward. The $5,000 AOCI gain is recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-10(b)により、AFS証券をトレーディングに移管する場合、移管日時点のAOCIの未実現損益は即時に純利益に再分類して認識する。証券は今後トレーディング・ポートフォリオで公正価値($95,000)で計上する。AOCIの$5,000の利得は損益で認識する。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Through Net Income Election
questions.stem: Entity B has a debt instrument that would normally be classified as AFS. Entity B elects the fair value option under ASC 825 at initial recognition. How are subsequent changes in fair value reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In other comprehensive income

choices.label: B
choices.text: In net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: In retained earnings directly

choices.label: D
choices.text: No subsequent measurement is required

questions.explanation: Under ASC 825-10-45-2, when the fair value option is elected, subsequent changes in fair value are recognized in net income. This is the case regardless of the original classification. The election is irrevocable and made on an instrument-by-instrument basis. The entity must disclose the reasons for electing the fair value option.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 825-10-45-2により、公正価値オプションを選択した場合、その後の公正価値変動は純利益で認識する。これは当初の分類にかかわらず適用される。選択は取消不能で金融商品ごとに行う。企業は公正価値オプション選択の理由を開示しなければならない。

questions.stem: Investor A acquires 30% of Investee B for $900,000 when Investee B's net assets have a book value of $2,400,000. The excess is attributable to equipment with a remaining life of 10 years. In Year 1, Investee B reports net income of $200,000. What is Investor A's equity method income?
questions.explanation: Step 1 — Basis difference: Investor A paid $900,000; share of book value = 30% × $2,400,000 = $720,000. Excess (basis difference) = $900,000 − $720,000 = $180,000, entirely attributable to equipment (10-year remaining life). Step 2 — Annual amortization: $180,000 / 10 = $18,000. Step 3 — Equity method income: Share of investee income = 30% × $200,000 = $60,000. Less basis difference amortization = $18,000. Equity method income = $60,000 − $18,000 = $42,000. Common mistakes: (A) $60,000 omits the amortization; (C) $48,000 uses an incorrect amortization figure; (D) $66,000 adds instead of subtracts the amortization.
questions.explanationJa: Step 1 — Basis差額：取得価額$900,000−持分簿価(30%×$2,400,000=$720,000)=$180,000（全額、設備の超過公正価値に帰属）。Step 2 — 年間償却：$180,000÷10年=$18,000。Step 3 — 持分法利益：投資先純利益の持分=30%×$200,000=$60,000。Basis差額償却を控除：$60,000−$18,000=$42,000。見積り変更なし。ASC 323-10-35-13に基づき、Basis差額は該当資産の残存耐用年数で規則的に償却する。

questions.subtopic: Equity Securities - Unrealized Gain/Loss in Income
questions.stem: A company holds equity securities with a cost of $150,000. At Year 1 end, fair value is $180,000. At Year 2 end, fair value is $165,000. What is the unrealized gain or loss recognized in Year 2 income?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-1, equity securities with readily determinable fair values are measured at fair value through net income each period. In Year 2, the fair value decreased from $180,000 to $165,000, resulting in a $15,000 unrealized loss recognized in net income. The comparison is always to the most recent carrying amount, not the original cost.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-1により、容易に決定可能な公正価値を持つ株式は毎期公正価値で測定し変動を純利益に認識。Year 2では$180,000→$165,000に減少し、$15,000の未実現損失を損益に計上。比較は常に直近の帳簿価額との差額であり原価との差額ではない。

questions.stem: A company holds a held-to-maturity debt security with an amortized cost of $200,000. Under ASC 326, the expected credit loss is estimated at $8,000. How should the impairment be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Write down the security to $192,000 directly

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record an allowance for credit losses of $8,000 with a corresponding credit loss expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize $8,000 in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: No impairment until the loss is realized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-30-1, HTM securities are subject to the CECL model. Expected credit losses are recorded through an allowance for credit losses (contra asset), not by writing down the amortized cost basis. The offset is credit loss expense in net income. This allowance approach preserves the amortized cost basis for interest income recognition purposes.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-1により、HTM証券はCECLモデルの対象。予想信用損失は償却原価を直接減額せず、貸倒引当金（評価勘定）を通じて記録。相手勘定は損益の信用損失費用。引当金方式により利息収入認識のための償却原価基準が維持される。

questions.stem: Investor C uses the equity method for its 40% investment in Investee D. The investment carrying amount is $500,000. Investee D declares and pays a cash dividend of $100,000. What is the effect on Investor C's financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dividend income of $40,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investment account decreases by $40,000; cash increases by $40,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect — dividends are not recognized under equity method

choices.label: D
choices.text: Investment account increases by $40,000

questions.explanation: Under the equity method (ASC 323-10-35-7), dividends received from the investee reduce the carrying amount of the investment, not recognized as income. Investor C receives 40% × $100,000 = $40,000 cash. The investment decreases from $500,000 to $460,000. Dividends under the equity method represent a return of investment, not a return on investment.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法（ASC 323-10-35-7）では被投資企業からの配当は投資の帳簿価額の減少であり収益認識しない。Investor Cの受取配当=40%×$100,000=$40,000。投資勘定は$500,000→$460,000に減少。配当は投資の回収（return of investment）であり投資収益ではない。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Security - Effective Interest Method
questions.stem: A company purchases a 5-year AFS bond with face value $100,000 at a premium of $4,000 (total cost $104,000). The stated rate is 6% and the effective rate is 5%. What is the interest income in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method, interest income is calculated by multiplying the carrying amount by the effective rate. Year 1 interest income = $104,000 × 5% = $5,200. The cash received is $100,000 × 6% = $6,000. The difference of $800 is premium amortization that reduces the carrying amount. This method produces a constant rate of return on the investment.
questions.explanationJa: 実効金利法では帳簿価額に実効利率を乗じて利息収入を計算。Year 1利息収入=$104,000×5%=$5,200。現金受取=$100,000×6%=$6,000。差額$800はプレミアム償却で帳簿価額を減額。この方法は投資に対する一定の収益率を生む。

questions.stem: A company holds equity securities without readily determinable fair value and uses the measurement alternative under ASC 321. The investment is carried at $100,000. An observable transaction for identical securities occurs at $130,000. What adjustment is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No adjustment — the measurement alternative does not permit upward adjustments

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase the investment to $130,000 and recognize a $30,000 gain in net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase the investment to $130,000 and recognize a $30,000 gain in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: Adjust to fair value only if the investment is impaired

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-2, the measurement alternative requires adjustment when there are observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments. The investment is adjusted to $130,000, and the $30,000 unrealized gain is recognized in net income. Both upward and downward adjustments are permitted under the measurement alternative.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-2により、測定代替法では同一または類似投資の秩序ある取引で観察可能な価格変動がある場合に調整が必要。投資を$130,000に調整し$30,000の未実現利得を純利益に認識。上方・下方の両方向の調整が認められる。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Investee Other Comprehensive Income
questions.stem: Investee E reports net income of $300,000 and other comprehensive income of $40,000 (unrealized gain on AFS securities). Investor F owns 25% of Investee E. How does Investor F account for the investee's OCI?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ignore the OCI — only net income affects the equity method

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record 25% of the OCI ($10,000) in Investor F's OCI and increase the investment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Include the $10,000 in equity method income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the OCI but do not adjust the investment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-18, an investor should recognize its proportionate share of the investee's OCI as part of its own OCI. Investor F records 25% × $40,000 = $10,000 as OCI and increases the investment carrying amount by $10,000. The investment is adjusted for the investor's share of all changes in the investee's equity, not just net income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-18により、投資企業は被投資企業のOCIの持分相当額を自社のOCIに認識。Investor F: 25%×$40,000=$10,000をOCIに計上し投資帳簿価額を$10,000増加。投資は純利益だけでなく被投資企業の資本変動の持分で調整される。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt - Credit Loss vs Non-Credit Loss
questions.stem: A company holds an AFS debt security with amortized cost of $100,000 and fair value of $85,000. The present value of expected cash flows is $92,000. What amounts are recognized for credit and non-credit losses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit loss $15,000 in net income; no OCI impact

choices.label: B
choices.text: Credit loss $8,000 in net income; non-credit loss $7,000 in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total loss $15,000 in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: Credit loss $8,000 in OCI; non-credit loss $7,000 in net income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-4, for AFS debt securities, the credit loss is measured as the difference between amortized cost and the present value of expected cash flows: $100,000 - $92,000 = $8,000, recognized through an allowance with expense in net income. The non-credit portion is the remaining decline: $92,000 - $85,000 = $7,000, recognized in OCI. Total decline = $15,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30-35-4により、AFS債券の信用損失=償却原価-予想キャッシュフロー現在価値=$100,000-$92,000=$8,000を引当金で純利益に認識。非信用部分=$92,000-$85,000=$7,000はOCIに認識。合計下落=$15,000。

questions.stem: Investor G owns 30% of Investee H and uses the equity method. Investor G sells half of its interest, reducing ownership to 15%, and no longer has significant influence. How should Investor G account for the retained 15% interest?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Continue using the equity method at the reduced percentage

choices.label: B
choices.text: Carry the retained interest at the equity method carrying amount at the date of loss of significant influence and apply ASC 321 prospectively

choices.label: C
choices.text: Remeasure the retained interest to fair value with gain/loss in income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Write off the retained interest to zero

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-36, when an investor loses significant influence, the equity method is discontinued. The retained investment is not remeasured to fair value (unlike a loss of control under ASC 810). Instead, the investment is carried at its equity method carrying amount on the date significant influence is lost, and prospectively accounted for under ASC 321 (equity securities).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-36により、重要な影響力を喪失した場合、持分法を中止する。保持投資は公正価値に再測定しない（ASC 810の支配喪失とは異なる）。喪失日の持分法帳簿価額で計上し、以後はASC 321（株式投資）に基づき会計処理する。

questions.stem: A company purchases trading securities for $50,000. At the end of Year 1, fair value is $58,000 and the company recognizes an $8,000 unrealized gain. In Year 2, the company sells the securities for $62,000. What is the realized gain recognized in Year 2?
questions.explanation: At the beginning of Year 2, the trading securities are carried at $58,000 (fair value from Year 1). When sold for $62,000, the realized gain is $62,000 - $58,000 = $4,000. The $8,000 unrealized gain was already recognized in Year 1 income. Total gain over the holding period is $12,000, but $8,000 was recognized in Year 1 and $4,000 in Year 2.
questions.explanationJa: Year 2期首の帳簿価額はYear 1末の公正価値$58,000。$62,000で売却時の実現利得=$62,000-$58,000=$4,000。$8,000の未実現利得はYear 1で既に認識済み。保有期間の総利得$12,000のうちYear 1に$8,000、Year 2に$4,000を認識。

questions.subtopic: Consolidation vs Equity Method - Variable Interest Entity
questions.stem: Company J holds a 10% equity interest in Entity K but is the primary beneficiary of Entity K, which is a variable interest entity (VIE). How should Company J account for its interest in Entity K?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cost method because ownership is below 20%

choices.label: B
choices.text: Equity method because of significant influence

choices.label: C
choices.text: Consolidation because Company J is the primary beneficiary

choices.label: D
choices.text: Fair value through net income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-15-14, the primary beneficiary of a VIE must consolidate the VIE regardless of the ownership percentage. The VIE model overrides the voting interest model. Even with only 10% equity ownership, Company J consolidates Entity K because it has the power to direct activities that most significantly affect the VIE's economic performance and the obligation to absorb losses or right to receive benefits.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 810-10-15-14により、VIEの主たる受益者は出資比率にかかわらずVIEを連結しなければならない。VIEモデルは議決権モデルに優先する。10%の出資でもCompany Jが経済的パフォーマンスに最も重要な影響を与える活動を指揮する権限と損失吸収/利益享受の義務/権利を有するため連結する。

questions.stem: A company purchases a $100,000 HTM bond at $103,000 with a 5% stated rate and 4% effective rate, paying interest semi-annually. What is the premium amortization for the first semi-annual period?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method: Semi-annual cash interest = $100,000 × 5% / 2 = $2,500. Semi-annual interest income = $103,000 × 4% / 2 = $103,000 × 2% = $2,060. Premium amortization = Cash interest − Interest income = $2,500 − $2,060 = $440. The premium amortization reduces the carrying amount of the bond each period, maintaining a constant effective yield of 4% annual (2% semi-annual).
questions.explanationJa: 実効金利法による計算：半期現金利息=$100,000×5%÷2=$2,500。半期利息収入=$103,000×4%÷2=$103,000×2%=$2,060。プレミアム償却=現金利息−利息収入=$2,500−$2,060=$440。プレミアム償却により毎期末の帳簿価額が減少し、実効利率4%（半期2%）が一定に保たれる。

questions.stem: Investor L owns 40% of Investee M (carrying amount $400,000). Investee M issues additional shares to third parties at a price above the per-share book value, diluting Investor L's interest to 30%. How should Investor L account for this dilution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a gain in net income for the increase in underlying equity per share

choices.label: B
choices.text: No accounting entry is required

choices.label: C
choices.text: Write down the investment proportionally

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a loss for the decrease in ownership percentage

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-40-1, when an investee issues shares at a price above the investor's per-share carrying amount, resulting in a dilution of the investor's interest, the investor recognizes a gain. The issuance effectively increases the investor's underlying equity in the investee on a per-share basis, even though the percentage ownership has decreased. The gain is calculated as the change in the investor's share of investee net assets.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-40-1により、被投資企業が投資企業の1株当たり帳簿価額を上回る価格で株式を発行し持分が希薄化した場合、投資企業は利得を認識する。発行により1株当たりの持分価値が増加するため。利得は被投資企業の純資産に対する持分の変動として計算。

questions.subtopic: Debt Security Reclassification - HTM to AFS
questions.stem: A company reclassifies a debt security from HTM to AFS. At the reclassification date, the amortized cost is $95,000 and the fair value is $98,000. How is the transfer accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record at $95,000 with no adjustment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record at $98,000 with $3,000 unrealized gain in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record at $98,000 with $3,000 gain in net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record at $95,000 with $3,000 deferred gain

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10(c), when HTM securities are reclassified to AFS, the security is transferred at fair value ($98,000). The unrealized holding gain of $3,000 ($98,000 - $95,000) is recognized in other comprehensive income (AOCI), not in net income. This is consistent with the normal treatment of AFS unrealized gains and losses.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-10(c)により、HTMからAFSへの移管時は公正価値$98,000で記録。未実現保有利得$3,000はOCI（AOCI）に認識し、純利益には計上しない。これはAFSの未実現損益の通常の処理と整合する。

questions.stem: A company uses the measurement alternative for equity securities without a readily determinable fair value. The investment is carried at $200,000. A qualitative assessment indicates impairment, and the fair value is estimated at $140,000. What is the journal entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Impairment Loss $60,000, Credit Investment $60,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit OCI $60,000, Credit Investment $60,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No entry until an observable transaction occurs

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Impairment Loss $60,000, Credit Allowance for Impairment $60,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-3, when qualitative assessment indicates impairment, the entity estimates fair value. If fair value is below carrying amount, an impairment loss of $200,000 - $140,000 = $60,000 is recognized in net income, and the investment is written down to $140,000. This write-down establishes a new cost basis. The impairment directly reduces the investment account, not through an allowance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-3により、定性的評価で減損の兆候がある場合は公正価値を見積もる。公正価値$140,000<帳簿価額$200,000のため、減損損失$60,000を純利益に認識し投資を$140,000に切下げ。これが新たな原価基準となる。引当金ではなく投資勘定を直接減額。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Upstream vs Downstream Transactions
questions.stem: Investor N owns 40% of Investee O. During the year, Investor N sells inventory (cost $60,000) to Investee O for $100,000. At year-end, 30% of this inventory remains unsold by Investee O. What is the unrealized profit that must be eliminated?
questions.explanation: This is a downstream transaction (investor sells to investee). Total gross profit = $100,000 - $60,000 = $40,000. Unsold portion = 30%, so unrealized profit = $40,000 × 30% = $12,000. For downstream transactions, the investor eliminates 100% of the unrealized profit in its remaining inventory (not just the investor's ownership percentage). The $12,000 is deferred against equity method income.
questions.explanationJa: 下流取引（投資企業→被投資企業）。総利益=$100,000-$60,000=$40,000。未販売割合30%の未実現利益=$40,000×30%=$12,000。下流取引では投資企業が未実現利益の100%（持分比率ではなく）を消去。$12,000は持分法利益から控除して繰延。

questions.stem: A company holds 1,000 shares of a publicly traded stock (cost method — less than 20% ownership). The stock declares a $2.00 per share cash dividend. How should the company record this?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Cash $2,000, Credit Dividend Income $2,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Cash $2,000, Credit Investment $2,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Dividend Receivable $2,000, Credit Unrealized Gain $2,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until the dividend is received

questions.explanation: For equity securities accounted for at fair value (under ASC 321), dividends received are recognized as dividend income in net income. The entry is: Debit Cash (or Dividend Receivable) $2,000, Credit Dividend Income $2,000. Unlike the equity method, dividends do not reduce the investment account. The investment continues to be measured at fair value independently of dividends.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321に基づく公正価値で測定する株式投資では、受取配当は純利益の配当収入として認識。仕訳: 現金$2,000(Dr)、配当収入$2,000(Cr)。持分法とは異なり、配当は投資勘定を減額しない。投資は配当とは独立して公正価値で測定を続ける。

questions.stem: A company sells an AFS debt security for $95,000. The amortized cost was $90,000 and AOCI included a $5,000 unrealized gain related to this security. What is recorded upon sale?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Realized gain of $5,000 in net income; reclassify $5,000 from AOCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: No gain — the $5,000 was already in AOCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: Realized gain of $10,000 in net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Realized gain of $5,000 in OCI

questions.explanation: When an AFS security is sold, the cumulative unrealized gain/loss in AOCI is reclassified (recycled) to net income as a realized gain or loss. Proceeds $95,000 - Amortized cost $90,000 = $5,000 realized gain recognized in income. The $5,000 is simultaneously removed from AOCI. This reclassification adjustment ensures the gain is not double-counted.
questions.explanationJa: AFS証券の売却時、AOCIの累積未実現損益を純利益に再分類（リサイクル）する。実現利得=売却額$95,000-償却原価$90,000=$5,000を損益に認識。同時にAOCIから$5,000を除去。この再分類調整により利得の二重計上を防止する。

questions.stem: Company P holds a 15% equity investment in Company Q (carried at $200,000 under ASC 321). Company P acquires an additional 55% for $750,000, obtaining control. The fair value of the previously held 15% interest is $225,000 on the acquisition date. How is the step acquisition treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Total investment is $950,000 (sum of $200,000 + $750,000)

choices.label: B
choices.text: The previously held 15% is remeasured to $225,000, and a $25,000 gain is recognized in income; total consideration is $975,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: The 15% is written down to zero and re-acquired at $225,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No remeasurement — carry forward the $200,000 basis

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-10-25-10, in a business combination achieved in stages (step acquisition), the acquirer remeasures its previously held equity interest at acquisition-date fair value and recognizes any resulting gain or loss in income. The 15% interest is remeasured from $200,000 to $225,000, recognizing a $25,000 gain. Total consideration = $225,000 + $750,000 = $975,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-10-25-10により、段階取得の企業結合では取得日に従前保有の持分を公正価値に再測定し、損益を認識。15%持分を$200,000→$225,000に再測定、$25,000の利得を計上。総対価=$225,000+$750,000=$975,000。

questions.stem: A company owns 18% of another entity's voting stock. Which of the following factors would indicate significant influence exists despite the below-20% ownership?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The investor purchases products from the investee

choices.label: B
choices.text: The investor has representation on the investee's board of directors

choices.label: C
choices.text: The investee has higher revenue than the investor

choices.label: D
choices.text: The investor and investee are in the same industry

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-15-6, indicators of significant influence include: representation on the board of directors, participation in policy-making processes, material intercompany transactions, interchange of managerial personnel, and technological dependency. Board representation (B) is one of the strongest indicators. Simply purchasing products (A) or being in the same industry (D) is not sufficient without additional factors.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-15-6により、重要な影響力の指標には: 取締役会への参加、方針決定への関与、重要な企業間取引、経営人材の交流、技術的依存が含まれる。取締役への就任(B)は最も強力な指標の一つ。製品購入(A)や同業種(D)だけでは不十分。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt - Amortized Cost Basis After Impairment
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Zeta Corp. purchased an AFS debt security for $100,000 (equal to par). At December 31, Year 1, the fair value declined to $85,000. Zeta determined $8,000 was attributable to credit loss and $7,000 to non-credit factors. After recording the impairment, what is the amortized cost basis of the security?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-4, for AFS debt securities, credit losses are recorded through an allowance for credit losses, which reduces the amortized cost basis for reporting purposes. The amortized cost basis itself remains at $100,000, but the net carrying amount is $100,000 - $8,000 allowance = $92,000. The non-credit portion ($7,000) is recognized in OCI. Therefore, the amortized cost basis net of the allowance is $92,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30-35-4により、AFS負債証券の信用損失は貸倒引当金を通じて記録される。償却原価$100,000 - 信用損失引当金$8,000 = $92,000が引当金控除後の純帳簿価額。非信用部分$7,000はOCIに認識。

questions.stem: Which of the following statements is correct regarding equity securities classified as available-for-sale under current U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Equity securities may be classified as available-for-sale with unrealized gains and losses in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: Under ASC 321, the AFS classification for equity securities was eliminated, and unrealized gains and losses are recognized in net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Equity securities with readily determinable fair values may be classified as AFS only if the entity elects the fair value option

choices.label: D
choices.text: Equity securities may be classified as AFS if held for more than one year

questions.explanation: ASC 321-10-35-1 requires that equity securities with readily determinable fair values be measured at fair value with changes recognized in net income (not OCI). The prior AFS classification for equity securities (which allowed unrealized gains/losses in OCI) was eliminated by ASU 2016-01. The only alternative for equities without readily determinable fair values is the measurement alternative under ASC 321-10-35-2.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-1により、容易に決定可能な公正価値のある持分証券は公正価値で測定し、変動は純利益に認識（OCIではない）。ASU 2016-01で持分証券のAFS区分は廃止された。容易に公正価値が決定できない持分証券には測定の代替(ASC 321-10-35-2)のみが利用可能。

questions.stem: Which of the following events would call into question an entity's intent to hold a debt security to maturity, potentially requiring reclassification out of the held-to-maturity category?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The security's fair value has increased above its amortized cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity sold HTM securities within the past two years for reasons other than those permitted under ASC 320

choices.label: C
choices.text: The issuer's credit rating was upgraded

choices.label: D
choices.text: Interest rates in the market have declined since the security was purchased

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-25-9, if an entity sells or transfers HTM securities for reasons other than those specifically permitted (e.g., significant credit deterioration, change in tax law, major business combination), the entity's intent to hold other securities to maturity is called into question ('tainting'). Sales within the current and two preceding fiscal years may taint the entire HTM portfolio. Fair value increases (A), credit upgrades (C), or interest rate changes (D) alone do not call intent into question.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-25-9により、許容される理由以外でHTM証券を売却・振替した場合、他のHTM証券を満期保有する意図が疑われる（テインティング）。過去2事業年度以内の売却はHTMポートフォリオ全体に影響する。公正価値上昇(A)、信用格上げ(C)、金利変動(D)だけでは意図は疑問視されない。

questions.stem: Delta Corp. sold $500,000 of held-to-maturity securities in Year 2 because it needed cash to fund an unexpected acquisition. The sale did not meet any of the permitted exceptions under ASC 320. What is the most likely consequence?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the sold securities must be reclassified; remaining HTM securities are unaffected

choices.label: B
choices.text: Delta must reclassify its entire HTM portfolio to either AFS or trading

choices.label: C
choices.text: Delta may continue to classify new purchases as HTM but must disclose the sale

choices.label: D
choices.text: The sale is retroactively reversed and the securities are restored to HTM

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-25-14, a sale of HTM securities that does not meet one of the permitted exceptions calls into question the entity's intent to hold its remaining HTM securities to maturity. The entity would generally be required to reclassify the entire remaining HTM portfolio to AFS (or trading). This 'tainting' effect discourages entities from using the HTM classification opportunistically.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-25-14により、許容例外に該当しないHTM証券の売却は、残りのHTM証券を満期まで保有する意図を疑問視させる。企業は通常、残りのHTMポートフォリオ全体をAFS（または売買目的）に再分類する必要がある。このテインティング効果は日和見的なHTM分類を防止する。

questions.subtopic: Trading - Portfolio Approach vs Individual Security
questions.stem: Alpha Corp. holds three trading securities at December 31, Year 1. Security X has an unrealized gain of $5,000, Security Y has an unrealized loss of $3,000, and Security Z has an unrealized gain of $2,000. What amount of net unrealized gain or loss should Alpha report in its Year 1 income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net unrealized gain of $4,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Unrealized gain of $7,000 and unrealized loss of $3,000, reported separately

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net unrealized gain of $4,000 or separately as $7,000 gain and $3,000 loss, depending on entity policy

choices.label: D
choices.text: No amount, as unrealized gains and losses on trading securities are reported in OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321-10-35-1, unrealized gains and losses on equity securities (including trading) are recognized in net income. ASC 320-10-45-1 permits reporting unrealized gains and losses on a net basis or separately (gross). Either a portfolio approach (net $4,000 = $5,000 + $2,000 - $3,000) or individual security approach is acceptable. Answer D is incorrect because trading security changes go through income, not OCI.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-1により、持分証券（売買目的含む）の未実現損益は純利益に認識。ASC 320-10-45-1では未実現損益を純額表示または総額表示（個別）のいずれも許容。純額$4,000（=$5,000+$2,000-$3,000）でも総額でも可。(D)は誤り—売買目的証券の変動はOCIではなく損益計上。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Investee Preferred Stock Adjustment
questions.stem: Gamma Corp. owns 40% of Omega Inc.'s common stock and uses the equity method. Omega reported net income of $500,000 for the year. Omega also has outstanding cumulative preferred stock with annual dividends of $50,000. What amount of equity method income should Gamma recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-4, the investor recognizes its proportionate share of the investee's earnings available to common stockholders. Omega's net income of $500,000 must be reduced by the preferred dividend of $50,000 to arrive at income available to common shareholders: $500,000 - $50,000 = $450,000. Gamma's share = 40% × $450,000 = $180,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-4により、投資者は被投資会社の普通株主帰属利益に対する持分を認識。Omega純利益$500,000 - 優先配当$50,000 = 普通株主帰属利益$450,000。Gammaの持分 = 40% × $450,000 = $180,000。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Change from Cost to Equity Method
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Apex Corp. acquired a 10% interest in Nova Inc. for $120,000 and accounted for it under ASC 321 at fair value through net income. On July 1, Year 2, Apex purchased an additional 15% for $200,000, giving it 25% ownership and significant influence. At July 1, Year 2, the fair value of the original 10% interest was $135,000 (already reflected in the carrying amount under ASC 321). What is Apex's equity method investment balance immediately after the second purchase on July 1, Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-33, when an investor obtains significant influence and adopts the equity method, the equity method is applied prospectively. The carrying amount of the previously held interest at the date significant influence is obtained becomes the new basis for the equity method investment. The 10% interest was carried at fair value ($135,000) under ASC 321. Upon adopting the equity method, this $135,000 carrying amount becomes the basis. Total investment = $135,000 (existing 10% at fair value) + $200,000 (new 15% purchase) = $335,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-33により、重要な影響力を取得し持分法を採用する場合、将来に向かって適用。影響力取得日の既存持分の帳簿価額が持分法投資の新たな基礎となる。10%持分はASC 321で公正価値$135,000で計上済み。持分法採用により、この$135,000が基礎となる。投資残高合計 = $135,000（既存10%の公正価値）+ $200,000（新規15%取得）= $335,000。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Change from Equity to Fair Value
questions.stem: Pine Corp. held a 30% interest in Oak Inc. accounted for under the equity method with a carrying amount of $450,000. Pine sold 15% of Oak's shares for $280,000, reducing its ownership to 15% and losing significant influence. The fair value of the remaining 15% interest on the date of sale is $260,000. What gain or loss should Pine recognize on the transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $55,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $90,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $80,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $35,000

questions.explanation: When an investor loses significant influence and discontinues the equity method, the investor must: (1) recognize a gain/loss on the shares sold, and (2) remeasure the retained interest at fair value. Carrying amount of 30% = $450,000, so carrying amount per percent = $15,000. Carrying amount of 15% sold = $225,000. Gain on sale = $280,000 - $225,000 = $55,000. Retained 15% carrying amount = $225,000; fair value = $260,000; remeasurement gain = $260,000 - $225,000 = $35,000. Total gain = $55,000 + $35,000 = $90,000.

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Investee Treasury Stock Transaction
questions.stem: River Corp. owns 25% (100,000 shares) of Lake Inc. and uses the equity method. Lake has 400,000 shares outstanding with a book value of $2,000,000. Lake repurchases 50,000 of its own shares from other shareholders at $6.00 per share. What is the impact on River's equity method investment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No effect on River's investment

choices.label: B
choices.text: River records a gain because its ownership percentage increased

choices.label: C
choices.text: River adjusts its investment for the change in its share of Lake's equity

choices.label: D
choices.text: River must purchase additional shares to maintain its 25% ownership

questions.explanation: When an investee repurchases its own shares, the investor's ownership percentage changes. Before: River owns 100,000 / 400,000 = 25%. After Lake repurchases 50,000 shares: River owns 100,000 / 350,000 = 28.57%. Lake's equity changes: original equity $2,000,000 minus treasury stock cost ($6.00 × 50,000 = $300,000) = $1,700,000. River's share of equity changes from 25% × $2,000,000 = $500,000 to 28.57% × $1,700,000 = $485,714. Under ASC 323-10-35-4, the investor adjusts its investment account for its share of changes in the investee's equity, which may result in a gain or loss recognized in income or an adjustment to additional paid-in capital.

questions.stem: Birch Corp. owns 30% of Cedar Inc. and uses the equity method with an investment balance of $600,000. Cedar receives a capital contribution of $200,000 from another investor, increasing Cedar's total equity. How should Birch account for this event?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No journal entry is required because Birch did not contribute

choices.label: B
choices.text: Birch records an increase to its investment for its share of the increase in Cedar's equity

choices.label: C
choices.text: Birch records a decrease to its investment because its ownership percentage is diluted

choices.label: D
choices.text: Birch recognizes dividend income of $60,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323-10-35-4, an equity method investor adjusts its investment account for its proportionate share of changes in the investee's equity. When Cedar receives a $200,000 capital contribution from another investor, Cedar's equity increases. Although Birch's percentage ownership may be diluted, its share of the increase in net assets is recognized. Birch records the net effect of the change in its proportionate share of Cedar's equity, which typically results in an increase to the investment account when the contribution is at or above the per-share book value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-4により、持分法投資者は被投資会社の資本変動に対する持分を調整。Cedarが他の投資者から$200,000の出資を受けると資本が増加。Birchの持分比率は希薄化する可能性があるが、純資産増加に対する持分を認識。出資が1株あたり簿価以上であれば、通常は投資勘定の増加となる。

questions.subtopic: Equity Securities - Net Unrealized Holding Gain/Loss Presentation
questions.stem: Under ASC 321, where are unrealized holding gains and losses on equity securities with readily determinable fair values presented in the financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In other comprehensive income (OCI)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Directly in retained earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: In net income within the income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: In a separate component of stockholders' equity

questions.explanation: ASC 321-10-35-1 requires that equity investments with readily determinable fair values be measured at fair value, with unrealized gains and losses recognized in net income in the current period's income statement. This was a significant change from the prior guidance under ASC 320, which allowed unrealized gains and losses on AFS equity securities to be reported in OCI. The OCI treatment (A) now applies only to AFS debt securities.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321-10-35-1により、容易に決定可能な公正価値のある持分証券は公正価値で測定し、未実現損益は当期の損益計算書で純利益に認識。これは旧ASC 320のAFS持分証券のOCI処理からの重要な変更。OCI処理(A)はAFS負債証券のみに適用。

questions.stem: An entity holds an investment measured using Level 3 inputs in the fair value hierarchy. Which of the following disclosures is specifically required for Level 3 measurements under ASC 820?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the fair value amount at the reporting date

choices.label: B
choices.text: A reconciliation of beginning and ending balances, including purchases, sales, gains, and losses

choices.label: C
choices.text: The quoted market price from an active market used to determine fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only the valuation technique used, without quantitative details

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-50-2(c), for recurring fair value measurements using significant unobservable inputs (Level 3), entities must provide a reconciliation from the opening balances to the closing balances, disclosing separately total gains/losses (realized and unrealized), purchases, sales, issuances, settlements, and transfers in and out of Level 3. This enhanced disclosure is required because Level 3 inputs involve the highest degree of management judgment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-50-2(c)により、重要な観察不能インプット（レベル3）を用いた経常的公正価値測定では、期首から期末残高への調整表の開示が必要。損益（実現・未実現）、購入、売却、発行、決済、レベル3への振替・からの振替を区分開示。レベル3は経営者の判断が最も大きいため強化された開示が要求される。

questions.stem: A company values a private equity investment using a discounted cash flow model with management's own assumptions about future revenue growth and discount rates. Under the ASC 820 fair value hierarchy, what level is this measurement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Level 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: Level 2

choices.label: C
choices.text: Level 3

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not within the fair value hierarchy because it uses management estimates

questions.explanation: Under ASC 820-10-35-52, Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability. These reflect the entity's own assumptions about assumptions that market participants would use in pricing the asset, developed based on the best information available in the circumstances. Management's own projections of future revenue growth and internally developed discount rates are unobservable inputs, placing this measurement in Level 3.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 820-10-35-52により、レベル3インプットは観察不能なインプット。市場参加者が使用する仮定について企業自身の仮定を反映する。経営者の将来収益成長予測や内部開発の割引率は観察不能インプットであり、レベル3に分類。(D)は誤り—経営者見積もりを使用しても公正価値ヒエラルキーの範囲内。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Option - Initial Election Irrevocability
questions.stem: Sigma Corp. elected the fair value option under ASC 825 for a particular debt investment at the time of initial recognition. Six months later, the market for similar securities became illiquid. Which of the following is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sigma may revoke the fair value option election and reclassify to amortized cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: Sigma must continue to measure the investment at fair value; the election is irrevocable

choices.label: C
choices.text: Sigma may temporarily suspend fair value measurement until the market becomes active again

choices.label: D
choices.text: Sigma must reclassify to Level 3 and use cost as a proxy for fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 825-10-25-4, the fair value option election is irrevocable once made. The election is made on an instrument-by-instrument basis at initial recognition (or other qualifying events), and the entity must continue to measure the instrument at fair value regardless of subsequent changes in market conditions. Market illiquidity does not permit revocation or suspension of the election.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 825-10-25-4により、公正価値オプションの選択は一度行うと取消不能。選択は当初認識時（または適格事象時）に商品ごとに行い、その後の市場状況の変化にかかわらず公正価値での測定を継続。市場の流動性低下は選択の取消や一時停止を許容しない。

questions.subtopic: Debt Security - Accrued Interest at Purchase
questions.stem: On March 1, Year 1, Metro Corp. purchased a 6% annual coupon bond (face $100,000) classified as AFS for $102,500 plus accrued interest. The bond pays interest semiannually on June 30 and December 31. What is the total cash paid by Metro on March 1?
questions.explanation: Metro pays the purchase price plus accrued interest from the last coupon date (December 31) to the purchase date (March 1). Accrued interest = $100,000 × 6% × 2/12 = $1,000. Total cash paid = $102,500 (purchase price) + $1,000 (accrued interest) = $103,500. The accrued interest is recorded as a receivable, not part of the investment cost.
questions.explanationJa: Metroは購入価格に加え、前回利払日（12月31日）から購入日（3月1日）までの経過利息を支払う。経過利息 = $100,000 × 6% × 2/12 = $1,000。支払総額 = $102,500（購入価格）+ $1,000（経過利息）= $103,500。経過利息は未収収益として計上し、投資原価には含めない。

questions.stem: Ace Corp. holds an HTM bond with a face value of $200,000, a carrying amount of $194,000, and remaining premium/discount amortization of $6,000. The issuer calls the bond at 102 (i.e., $204,000). What gain or loss does Ace recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $10,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $4,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $6,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $6,000

questions.explanation: When a bond is called before maturity, the investor recognizes the difference between the call price received and the carrying amount of the investment. Call price = $200,000 × 102% = $204,000. Carrying amount = $194,000. Gain = $204,000 - $194,000 = $10,000. The remaining discount of $6,000 is effectively accelerated into income as part of this gain calculation.
questions.explanationJa: 社債が満期前に償還される場合、投資者は受取償還価額と帳簿価額の差額を認識。償還価額 = $200,000 × 102% = $204,000。帳簿価額 = $194,000。利得 = $204,000 - $194,000 = $10,000。残存割引$6,000はこの利得計算の一部として実質的に加速償却される。

questions.subtopic: Debt Security - Effective Interest Rate Calculation
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Nile Corp. purchases a 3-year bond with a face value of $100,000 and a stated rate of 5% (annual coupon) for $97,277. The effective interest rate is 6%. What is the interest income Nile recognizes for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method, interest income = carrying amount × effective interest rate. Year 1 interest income = $97,277 × 6% = $5,836.62, rounded to $5,837. The stated coupon of $5,000 ($100,000 × 5%) is the cash received, and the difference of $837 is the discount amortization added to the carrying amount.
questions.explanationJa: 実効金利法では、利息収益 = 帳簿価額 × 実効金利。Year 1の利息収益 = $97,277 × 6% = $5,836.62 ≈ $5,837。額面クーポン$5,000（$100,000 × 5%）が現金受取額で、差額$837が帳簿価額に加算される割引償却額。

questions.stem: Parent Corp. owns 100% of Sub Corp. and uses the cost method on its separate (parent-only) financial statements. Sub reported net income of $300,000 and declared dividends of $80,000 during Year 1. What amount does Parent recognize as income from Sub on its separate financial statements?
questions.explanation: Under the cost method for parent-only (separate) financial statements, the parent recognizes income from the subsidiary only when dividends are declared by the subsidiary. Parent recognizes dividend income of $80,000. The subsidiary's net income of $300,000 would only affect the parent's statements through consolidation or the equity method, not the cost method. Under the cost method, the investment account remains at original cost unless impaired.
questions.explanationJa: 親会社単独（個別）財務諸表で原価法を使用する場合、子会社からの収益は配当が宣言された時のみ認識。親会社は配当収益$80,000を認識。子会社の純利益$300,000は連結や持分法でのみ親会社に影響し、原価法では影響しない。原価法では投資勘定は減損がない限り取得原価のまま。

questions.subtopic: Consolidated vs Equity - Loss of Significant Influence
questions.stem: Horizon Corp. owned 35% of Vista Inc. and used the equity method. Horizon sold 20% of Vista's shares, reducing its ownership to 15% and losing significant influence. The carrying amount of the 35% interest was $700,000 at the date of sale. Horizon received $500,000 for the 20% sold. The fair value of the remaining 15% is $280,000. What is the total gain or loss Horizon recognizes?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $20,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $180,000

questions.explanation: Carrying amount per percentage point = $700,000 / 35 = $20,000. Carrying amount of 20% sold = 20 × $20,000 = $400,000. Gain on sale = $500,000 - $400,000 = $100,000. Remaining 15% carrying amount = 15 × $20,000 = $300,000. Remeasurement to fair value = $280,000 - $300,000 = ($20,000) loss. Total gain = $100,000 - $20,000 = $80,000.

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt - Sale Before Maturity Gain/Loss
questions.stem: Clark Corp. sells an AFS debt security for $95,000. At the date of sale, the security has an amortized cost of $90,000 and a fair value of $95,000, with $5,000 of unrealized gain in accumulated OCI. What is the realized gain Clark recognizes in net income upon sale?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 320-10-35-10, when an AFS debt security is sold, the previously unrealized gain or loss in AOCI is reclassified to net income as a realized gain or loss. Sale proceeds = $95,000; amortized cost = $90,000; realized gain = $95,000 - $90,000 = $5,000. The $5,000 previously in AOCI is reclassified out of OCI and into earnings. This is the reclassification adjustment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-10により、AFS負債証券を売却する際、AOCIの未実現損益は純利益に実現損益として組替え。売却代金$95,000 - 償却原価$90,000 = 実現利得$5,000。AOCIの$5,000はOCIから利益に組替え（reclassification adjustment）。

questions.stem: Under current U.S. GAAP (ASC 320), which of the following statements regarding the transfer of a debt security from the trading category to the available-for-sale category is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Transfers from trading to AFS are permitted and the security is transferred at fair value, with no gain or loss recognized at transfer because unrealized gains/losses were already in income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Transfers from trading to AFS are prohibited under all circumstances

choices.label: C
choices.text: Transfers from trading to AFS require reversal of previously recognized unrealized gains/losses

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transfers from trading to AFS are recorded at amortized cost, ignoring fair value changes
