Year 2: interest income = $95,700 × 6% = $5,742; cash coupon = $5,000; amortization = $742; carrying amount = $95,700 + $742 = $96,442. $97,000 results from straight-line amortization ($5,000 discount ÷ 5 years × 2 years), which is not GAAP for this purpose. $95,700 is the carrying amount after only Year 1. $96,400 applies the Year 1 amortization amount again in Year 2 instead of recomputing on the higher carrying amount.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-30の実効金利法で計算します。Year 1: 利息収益 = $95,000 × 6% = $5,700、クーポン $5,000、償却 $700 → 簿価 $95,700。Year 2: 利息収益 = $95,700 × 6% = $5,742、クーポン $5,000、償却 $742 → 簿価 $96,442。定額法（$1,000 × 2年で $97,000）は原則不可。$95,700 はYear 1末の簿価、$96,400 はYear 2もYear 1と同額の償却を使った誤りです。

questions.stem: Foundry Corp. purchases an HTM debt security that, as of the acquisition date, has experienced a more-than-insignificant deterioration in credit quality since origination (a purchased credit-deteriorated, or PCD, asset). Under ASC 326, how is the expected credit loss at acquisition recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As credit loss expense in earnings on the acquisition date

choices.label: B
choices.text: By adding the initial allowance for credit losses to the purchase price to establish the amortized cost basis, with no Day 1 credit loss expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: In other comprehensive income until the loss is realized

choices.label: D
choices.text: No allowance is recorded because the credit deterioration is already reflected in the purchase price

questions.explanation: PCD assets use a gross-up approach: the initial allowance for expected credit losses is added to the purchase price to determine the amortized cost basis at acquisition, so no credit loss expense is recognized in earnings on Day 1. Subsequent changes in expected credit losses adjust the allowance through earnings. Recognizing Day 1 expense is the treatment PCD accounting is designed to avoid (it would double count the discount already priced in). The loss is not parked in OCI, and an allowance is recorded—the gross-up makes the discount attributable to credit explicit rather than leaving it embedded in yield.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-13により、PCD資産は「グロスアップ・アプローチ」を用います。取得時の期待信用損失引当金を購入価格に加算して償却原価を算定するため、取得日（Day 1）に信用損失費用は計上されません。以後の期待信用損失の変動は引当金を通じて損益に計上します。Day 1費用の認識は購入価格に既に織り込まれた信用ディスカウントの二重計上となるため、PCD会計はこれを回避する設計です。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Losses Beyond Zero When Support Is Committed
questions.stem: Quincy Corp.'s equity method investment in Reel Inc. has been reduced to zero by Reel's accumulated losses. Reel continues to incur losses. Under which of the following circumstances should Quincy continue to recognize its share of Reel's additional losses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Whenever the investee's losses continue, regardless of the investor's obligations

choices.label: B
choices.text: Never; equity method losses can never reduce the investment below zero under any circumstances

choices.label: C
choices.text: When Quincy has guaranteed obligations of Reel or is otherwise committed to provide further financial support

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only if Quincy plans to sell the investment within one year

questions.explanation: Equity method loss recognition normally stops when the investment (including advances that are in-substance part of the net investment) reaches zero. However, the investor continues to recognize losses—recording a liability—if it has guaranteed the investee's obligations or is otherwise committed to provide further financial support, or if the investee's imminent return to profitability appears assured. Recognizing losses unconditionally or refusing to recognize them under all circumstances are both incorrect, and an intent to sell does not trigger continued loss recognition.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-35-19〜20により、持分法損失の認識は通常、投資簿価（実質的な純投資を含む）がゼロになった時点で停止します。ただし、投資者が被投資会社の債務を保証している場合や追加的な財務支援をコミットしている場合は、負債を計上して損失認識を継続します。無条件に認識を続ける処理や、いかなる場合もゼロ未満を認めない処理はいずれも誤りで、売却予定の有無は判断基準ではありません。

questions.stem: Under ASC 825, the fair value option may NOT be elected for which of the following items?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An investment that would otherwise be accounted for under the equity method

choices.label: B
choices.text: A held-to-maturity debt security at initial recognition

choices.label: C
choices.text: A firm commitment involving only financial instruments

choices.label: D
choices.text: An investment in a subsidiary that the entity is required to consolidate

questions.explanation: ASC 825-10-15-5 specifically excludes from the fair value option an investment in a subsidiary that the entity is required to consolidate, along with items such as pension obligations, lease assets and liabilities, and demand deposit liabilities. Equity method investments, recognized financial assets including debt securities at initial recognition, and firm commitments involving only financial instruments are all eligible items under ASC 825-10-15-4.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 825-10-15-5は、公正価値オプションの適用除外項目として「連結が要求される子会社への投資」を明示しています（ほかに年金債務、リース資産・負債、要求払預金負債等）。一方、持分法投資、当初認識時の金融資産（負債証券を含む）、金融商品のみが関係する確定約定はASC 825-10-15-4の適格項目です。

questions.subtopic: Equity Securities - Partial Sale and Year-End Remeasurement
questions.stem: In Year 1, Cobble Corp. purchased 10,000 shares of Trent Inc. (a less than 5% interest with readily determinable fair value) for $20 per share. At December 31, Year 1, the stock traded at $23. During Year 2, Cobble sold 4,000 shares at $25 per share, and at December 31, Year 2, the remaining 6,000 shares traded at $21. What net amount should Cobble report in Year 2 net income from this investment (excluding dividends)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $4,000 net loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: $8,000 net gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: $20,000 net gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: $12,000 net loss

questions.explanation: Under ASC 321, the shares were remeasured to $23 at the end of Year 1 (that gain was Year 1 income). In Year 2, the realized gain on the 4,000 shares sold is measured against the $23 carrying amount: ($25 − $23) × 4,000 = $8,000 gain. The remaining 6,000 shares are remeasured from $23 to $21: ($21 − $23) × 6,000 = $(12,000) unrealized loss. Net Year 2 effect = $8,000 − $12,000 = $(4,000) net loss. $8,000 ignores the year-end remeasurement of the retained shares. $20,000 incorrectly measures the sale gain against the original $20 cost (double counting the Year 1 gain). $12,000 loss ignores the gain on the shares sold.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 321では株式は毎期末公正価値に再測定され、Year 1末の簿価は $23/株です。Year 2の売却益は簿価ベースで ($25 − $23) × 4,000株 = $8,000。残存6,000株は $23 → $21 に再測定され未実現損失 △$12,000。Year 2の純額 = $8,000 − $12,000 = △$4,000 の損失です。取得原価 $20 と比較した $20,000 はYear 1に計上済みの利益の二重計上となる誤りです。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt - Unrealized Gain Reported Net of Tax in OCI
questions.stem: During Year 1, Wharf Corp.'s AFS debt securities increased in fair value by $50,000 above amortized cost (no credit losses). Wharf's income tax rate is 25%. What amount should Wharf report in other comprehensive income for Year 1 related to this unrealized gain?
questions.explanation: Components of other comprehensive income are reported net of related tax effects (or gross with the tax effect shown as a single separate line). The unrealized holding gain of $50,000 carries a deferred tax effect of $50,000 × 25% = $12,500, so the net-of-tax amount in OCI is $37,500. $50,000 is the pretax amount, $12,500 is only the tax effect, and $0 is wrong because unrealized gains on AFS debt securities are recognized in OCI each period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 220-10-45-12により、OCIの各内訳項目は関連する税効果控除後（純額）で表示するか、税効果を一括表示します。未実現利益 $50,000 に対する繰延税金は $50,000 × 25% = $12,500。OCI計上額（税引後）= $50,000 − $12,500 = $37,500 です。税引前の $50,000、税効果のみの $12,500 は誤りです。

questions.subtopic: HTM - Balance Sheet Classification as Current or Noncurrent
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Kiln Corp. holds an HTM debt security that matures on June 30, Year 2. On a classified balance sheet, how should Kiln present this security?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a noncurrent asset at fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a noncurrent asset at amortized cost, because HTM securities are always noncurrent

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a current asset at amortized cost, net of any allowance for credit losses

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a current asset at fair value

questions.explanation: HTM debt securities are carried at amortized cost (net of any allowance for credit losses under ASC 326), not fair value. On a classified balance sheet, a security maturing within twelve months of the balance sheet date is presented as a current asset. HTM securities are not automatically noncurrent—classification follows the maturity (or expected realization) date. Fair value presentation applies to trading and AFS securities, not HTM.
questions.explanationJa: HTM負債証券はASC 320-10-35-1により償却原価（ASC 326の信用損失引当金控除後）で計上され、公正価値では測定しません。区分式貸借対照表では、決算日から12か月以内（Year 2の6月30日）に満期が到来するため流動資産に表示します。HTMが常に固定資産になるわけではなく、満期日に応じて流動・固定を区分します。公正価値表示は売買目的・AFSに適用されるものです。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Partial Sale Retaining Significant Influence
questions.stem: Sequoia Corp. owns 40% of Laurel Inc. with an equity method carrying amount of $800,000. Sequoia sells one-fourth of its holding (a 10% interest in Laurel) for $230,000 and retains significant influence over Laurel with its remaining 30% interest. What gain or loss should Sequoia recognize on the sale, and how should it account for the retained interest?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No gain or loss; the proceeds reduce the carrying amount of the investment

choices.label: B
choices.text: $230,000 gain; remeasure the retained interest to fair value

choices.label: C
choices.text: $30,000 gain; remeasure the retained 30% interest to fair value through net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: $30,000 gain; continue the equity method on the retained 30% interest at its $600,000 carrying amount

questions.explanation: The carrying amount allocated to the portion sold is $800,000 × 1/4 = $200,000, so the realized gain is $230,000 − $200,000 = $30,000, recognized in net income. Because Sequoia retains significant influence, it continues to apply the equity method to the remaining 30% interest at its carrying amount of $600,000—no remeasurement occurs. Treating the proceeds as a pure reduction of carrying amount ignores the realized gain. $230,000 treats the entire proceeds as gain. Remeasuring the retained interest to fair value applies only when significant influence is lost and the investment moves to ASC 321, which is not the case here.
questions.explanationJa: 売却部分に配分される簿価 = $800,000 × 1/4 = $200,000。売却益 = $230,000 − $200,000 = $30,000（純利益に計上）。重要な影響を保持しているため、残存30%持分（簿価 $600,000）には引き続き持分法を適用し、公正価値への再測定は行いません（ASC 323-10-35-35）。再測定が必要なのは重要な影響を喪失してASC 321へ移行する場合です。

questions.subtopic: AFS Portfolio - Net Unrealized Loss Across Multiple Securities
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Ledger Corp. holds two AFS debt securities, neither of which has any credit loss: Security One with an amortized cost of $100,000 and a fair value of $104,000, and Security Two with an amortized cost of $150,000 and a fair value of $143,000. What net amount should Ledger report in other comprehensive income for Year 1 (ignoring taxes), assuming both securities were purchased during Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $3,000 net unrealized loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: $7,000 net unrealized loss

choices.label: C
choices.text: $4,000 net unrealized gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: $11,000 net unrealized loss

questions.explanation: Each AFS security is remeasured to fair value with the unrealized holding gain or loss reported in OCI: Security One has a $4,000 unrealized gain ($104,000 − $100,000) and Security Two has a $7,000 unrealized loss ($143,000 − $150,000). The net OCI effect is $4,000 − $7,000 = $(3,000) net unrealized loss, and the securities are reported on the balance sheet at their total fair value of $247,000. $7,000 counts only the loss security, $4,000 counts only the gain security, and $11,000 incorrectly adds the gain and loss together as if both were losses.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320-10-35-1により、AFS証券は個々に公正価値評価し、未実現損益をOCIに計上します。証券1: $104,000 − $100,000 = +$4,000、証券2: $143,000 − $150,000 = △$7,000。OCI純額 = △$3,000の未実現損失。貸借対照表では合計公正価値 $247,000 で表示します。損失側のみの $7,000、利益側のみの $4,000、両者を加算した $11,000 は誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Upstream Land Sale Profit Elimination
questions.stem: Tarn Corp. owns 30% of Fjord Inc. and uses the equity method. During Year 1, Fjord sold land to Tarn and recognized a gain of $50,000; Tarn still holds the land at year-end. Fjord reported Year 1 net income of $300,000 (which includes the land gain). What equity method income should Tarn report for Year 1?
questions.explanation: In this upstream sale, Tarn must eliminate its share of the intra-entity gain on the land because the land remains within the investor-investee group: $50,000 × 30% = $15,000. Unlike depreciable assets, land is not depreciated, so none of the deferred gain is realized over time—it remains deferred until Tarn sells the land to an outside party. Equity method income = (30% × $300,000) − $15,000 = $90,000 − $15,000 = $75,000. $90,000 ignores the elimination. $60,000 incorrectly eliminates the entire $50,000 gain rather than the investor's 30% share. $85,000 has no supportable basis in the facts.
questions.explanationJa: アップストリーム取引（被投資会社→投資者）の未実現利益は、ASC 323-10-35-7により投資者持分相当を消去します。消去額 = $50,000 × 30% = $15,000。土地は減価償却されないため、外部に売却されるまで利益は実現せず繰延が継続します。持分法利益 = 30% × $300,000 − $15,000 = $75,000。消去を無視した $90,000、利益全額 $50,000 を消去した $60,000 は誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Debt vs Equity - HTM Classification Eligibility
questions.stem: Which of the following best explains why an investment in common stock of another entity cannot be classified as held-to-maturity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Common stock investments are always held for trading purposes

choices.label: B
choices.text: Common stock must always be accounted for under the equity method

choices.label: C
choices.text: Common stock has no maturity date, so the holder cannot have the intent and ability to hold it to maturity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Common stock fair values are too volatile to permit amortized cost measurement

questions.explanation: The held-to-maturity category under ASC 320 applies only to debt securities because classification requires the positive intent and ability to hold the security to maturity. Equity securities such as common stock are perpetual instruments with no maturity date, so the held-to-maturity criterion can never be met. The idea that all stock is held for trading is false—equity securities are simply measured under ASC 321. The equity method applies only when significant influence exists, not to all stock holdings. Price volatility is not the conceptual reason for the exclusion; the absence of a maturity date is.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320の満期保有（HTM）区分は負債証券にのみ適用される。HTM分類には「満期まで保有する積極的な意図と能力」が必要だが、普通株式などの持分証券には満期が存在しないため、この要件を満たすことが概念上不可能である。すべての株式が売買目的というわけではなく、持分証券は単にASC 321で測定される。持分法は重要な影響力がある場合にのみ適用される。価格変動の大きさが除外の理由ではなく、満期日が存在しないことが理由である。

questions.stem: Hollis Corp. owns 30% of Marsh Inc. and accounts for the investment using the equity method. How should Hollis present its share of Marsh's net income in its income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Combined with Hollis's revenue from customers, with disclosure in the notes

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a single line item, such as equity in earnings of investee

choices.label: C
choices.text: By consolidating each of Marsh's revenue and expense line items proportionately

choices.label: D
choices.text: As dividend income equal to dividends declared by Marsh during the period

questions.explanation: The equity method is often described as a one-line consolidation. The investor reports its proportionate share of the investee's net income as a single line item (commonly labeled equity in earnings of investee) in its income statement. Combining the amount with revenue from customers misstates the nature of the income. Proportionate consolidation of each revenue and expense line is not permitted under U.S. GAAP for equity method investments. Recording dividend income describes the treatment for investments without significant influence; under the equity method, dividends reduce the investment account and are not income.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法はしばしば「一行連結（one-line consolidation）」と呼ばれる。投資会社は被投資会社の純利益に対する持分相当額を、損益計算書上の単一の表示科目（equity in earnings of investee 等）として報告する。顧客からの収益と合算する処理は収益の性質を誤表示する。収益・費用を持分割合で各行に取り込む比例連結は米国GAAPの持分法では認められない。配当を収益計上する処理は重要な影響力がない投資の処理であり、持分法では配当は投資勘定の減額となり収益にはならない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 321, an equity security has a readily determinable fair value if which of the following conditions is met?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sales prices or bid-and-asked quotations are currently available on a securities exchange registered with the SEC or in an over-the-counter market with publicly reported prices

choices.label: B
choices.text: The issuer provides audited financial statements within 90 days of year-end

choices.label: C
choices.text: An independent appraiser has estimated the security's value within the past 12 months

choices.label: D
choices.text: The investor can estimate fair value using a discounted cash flow model with internally developed assumptions

questions.explanation: An equity security has a readily determinable fair value when sales prices or bid-and-asked quotations are currently available on an SEC-registered securities exchange or in an over-the-counter market where prices are publicly reported. The availability of audited financial statements relates to the quality of the issuer's reporting, not to the observability of a market price. An appraisal is an estimate, not a quoted market price. A discounted cash flow model with internal assumptions is a Level 3 valuation technique; the ability to model a value does not make fair value readily determinable. Securities lacking a readily determinable fair value may be measured using the measurement alternative.
questions.explanationJa: 持分証券の公正価値が「容易に算定可能（readily determinable）」であるのは、SEC登録の証券取引所または価格が公表される店頭市場で、売買価格や気配値が現在入手できる場合である。監査済財務諸表の入手可能性は発行体の報告の質の問題であり、市場価格の観察可能性とは関係ない。鑑定評価は見積りであって市場の公表価格ではない。内部の仮定を用いた割引キャッシュ・フロー・モデルはレベル3の評価技法であり、価値をモデル化できることは公正価値が容易に算定可能であることを意味しない。容易に算定可能な公正価値がない証券には測定代替（measurement alternative）を適用できる。

questions.stem: Dover Corp. purchases debt securities that it intends to sell within the next several days in order to generate profits from short-term differences in price. How should Dover classify these debt securities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Held-to-maturity, because the securities are debt instruments

choices.label: B
choices.text: Available-for-sale, because the securities will be sold before maturity

choices.label: C
choices.text: As equity securities measured at fair value through net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Trading, because the securities are bought and held principally for sale in the near term

questions.explanation: Debt securities bought and held principally for the purpose of selling them in the near term to profit from short-term price movements are classified as trading securities under ASC 320. They are measured at fair value with unrealized gains and losses in net income. Held-to-maturity classification is wrong because Dover has no intent to hold to maturity. Available-for-sale is a residual category for debt securities that are neither trading nor held-to-maturity; when the near-term sale intent exists, trading classification applies. The instruments are debt, not equity, so ASC 321 does not govern them.
questions.explanationJa: 短期的な価格変動から利益を得る目的で近い将来に売却するために購入・保有する負債証券は、ASC 320の下でトレーディング（売買目的）有価証券に分類される。公正価値で測定し、未実現損益は純利益に計上する。満期まで保有する意図がないため満期保有区分は誤り。売却可能（AFS）はトレーディングにも満期保有にも該当しない負債証券の残余区分であり、近い将来の売却意図がある場合はトレーディング分類が適用される。本問の証券は負債証券であり持分証券ではないため、ASC 321の適用対象ではない。

questions.stem: Which of the following is required for an entity to classify a debt security as held-to-maturity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The intent to hold the security for an indefinite period of time

choices.label: B
choices.text: The positive intent and the ability to hold the security to maturity

choices.label: C
choices.text: A contractual restriction that prohibits the entity from selling the security

choices.label: D
choices.text: The intent to hold the security until its fair value recovers to at least its amortized cost

questions.explanation: ASC 320 requires both the positive intent and the ability to hold a debt security to maturity for held-to-maturity classification. An intent to hold for an indefinite period is specifically insufficient—indefinite is not the same as to maturity. No contractual sale restriction is required; the criterion concerns the holder's own intent and financial ability. Intending to hold only until fair value recovers is an intent tied to price movements, which is inconsistent with the held-to-maturity criterion.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320では、負債証券を満期保有に分類するには「満期まで保有する積極的な意図」と「保有する能力」の両方が必要である。「無期限に保有する意図」では不十分とされており、無期限と満期までは同義ではない。売却を禁止する契約上の制限は要件ではなく、要件は保有者自身の意図と財務的能力に関するものである。「公正価値が償却原価まで回復するまで保有する意図」は価格変動に連動した意図であり、満期保有の要件と整合しない。

questions.stem: Walden Corp. holds an available-for-sale debt security with an amortized cost of $400,000 and a fair value of $350,000 at December 31, Year 1. On that date, management decides that it intends to sell the security early in Year 2. No allowance for credit losses had previously been recorded. How should Walden account for the decline in value at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Write the security down to $350,000 and recognize a $50,000 loss in net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record a $50,000 unrealized loss in other comprehensive income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record an allowance for credit losses of $50,000 with the charge split between earnings and other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Make no entry until the security is actually sold in Year 2

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30, when an entity intends to sell an impaired available-for-sale debt security (or it is more likely than not that it will be required to sell before recovery), the security's amortized cost basis is written down to fair value through earnings. Walden recognizes the full $400,000 − $350,000 = $50,000 loss in net income, and no allowance for credit losses is used. Recording the decline in other comprehensive income applies only when the entity does not intend to sell and the decline is not credit-related. The allowance approach, which separates credit losses (earnings) from non-credit declines (OCI), applies only when there is no intent or requirement to sell. Waiting until the actual sale would improperly defer a loss that must be recognized once the intent to sell exists.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30では、減損したAFS負債証券を売却する意図がある場合（または回復前に売却を要求される可能性が50%超の場合）、償却原価を公正価値まで直接切り下げ、損失を純利益に計上する。Waldenは$400,000 − $350,000 = $50,000の損失全額を純利益に認識し、信用損失引当金は使用しない。その他の包括利益（OCI）に計上する処理は、売却意図がなく、かつ下落が信用関連でない場合にのみ適用される。信用損失（純利益）と非信用要因（OCI）に分ける引当金アプローチは、売却の意図も要求もない場合の処理である。実際の売却まで処理を繰り延べるのは、売却意図が生じた時点で認識すべき損失の不当な繰延べとなる。

questions.stem: Keller Corp. transfers a debt security from the held-to-maturity category to the trading category. At the date of transfer, the security has an amortized cost of $190,000 and a fair value of $198,000. What is the effect of the transfer on Keller's net income at the transfer date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No effect; the $8,000 difference is recorded in other comprehensive income

choices.label: B
choices.text: No effect; the security is transferred at its amortized cost of $190,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: An $8,000 unrealized gain is recognized in net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: An $8,000 gain is deferred and amortized over the security's remaining life

questions.explanation: A security transferred into the trading category is recorded at fair value at the transfer date, and any unrealized holding gain or loss is recognized in earnings immediately. The security is recorded at $198,000, and the $198,000 − $190,000 = $8,000 unrealized gain goes to net income. Recording the difference in other comprehensive income describes a transfer from held-to-maturity to available-for-sale, not to trading. Carrying over the $190,000 amortized cost would understate the security, since trading securities must be carried at fair value. Deferring and amortizing the gain over the remaining life applies to the AOCI balance in a transfer from available-for-sale to held-to-maturity, not here.
questions.explanationJa: トレーディング区分へ振り替えられた証券は振替日の公正価値で計上し、未実現保有損益は直ちに純利益に認識する。証券は$198,000で計上され、$198,000 − $190,000 = $8,000の未実現利益が純利益に計上される。差額をその他の包括利益に計上するのは満期保有からAFSへの振替の処理であり、トレーディングへの振替には当てはまらない。償却原価$190,000のまま引き継ぐのは、公正価値で計上すべきトレーディング証券の過小表示となる。残存期間にわたる利得の繰延・償却は、AFSから満期保有への振替時のAOCI残高の処理であり本問には該当しない。

questions.subtopic: HTM - Effective Interest Carrying Amount Year 1
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Brandt Corp. purchased a 4-year, $600,000 face value bond for $564,000 and classified it as held-to-maturity. The bond pays interest annually each December 31 at a stated rate of 6%, and the effective interest rate is 8%. What is the carrying amount of the bond at December 31, Year 1, after the receipt of the first interest payment?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method, Year 1 interest income = $564,000 × 8% = $45,120. Cash interest received = $600,000 × 6% = $36,000. Discount amortization = $45,120 − $36,000 = $9,120. Carrying amount at December 31, Year 1 = $564,000 + $9,120 = $573,120. The $573,000 figure results from straight-line amortization of the $36,000 discount over 4 years ($9,000 per year), which is not the required method. The $564,000 figure ignores amortization entirely, and $600,000 is the face value, which the carrying amount will not reach until maturity.
questions.explanationJa: 実効金利法では、Year 1の受取利息 = $564,000 × 8% = $45,120。現金受取利息 = $600,000 × 6% = $36,000。ディスカウント償却額 = $45,120 − $36,000 = $9,120。Year 1末の帳簿価額 = $564,000 + $9,120 = $573,120。$573,000はディスカウント$36,000を4年間で定額償却（年$9,000）した結果であり、要求される方法ではない。$564,000は償却を全く反映しておらず、$600,000は額面金額であり満期まで帳簿価額は到達しない。

questions.subtopic: Bond Purchase Price - Present Value Computation
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Fenwick Corp. purchases a 3-year bond with a face value of $100,000 and a stated annual interest rate of 4%, with interest paid each December 31. The market (effective) rate for similar bonds is 6%. The present value of $1 at 6% for 3 periods is 0.840, and the present value of an ordinary annuity of $1 at 6% for 3 periods is 2.673. What is the purchase price of the bond?
questions.explanation: The price of a bond equals the present value of its future cash flows discounted at the market rate. Annual cash interest = $100,000 × 4% = $4,000. Price = ($4,000 × 2.673) + ($100,000 × 0.840) = $10,692 + $84,000 = $94,692. Because the stated rate (4%) is below the market rate (6%), the bond sells at a discount below face value. The $84,000 amount is the present value of the principal only, omitting the interest payments. The $100,000 amount is the face value, which would be the price only if the stated and market rates were equal. The $96,000 amount adds the undiscounted interest payments ($12,000) to the present value of the principal, failing to discount the coupons.
questions.explanationJa: 債券の価格は将来キャッシュ・フローを市場利子率で割り引いた現在価値に等しい。年間の現金利息 = $100,000 × 4% = $4,000。価格 = ($4,000 × 2.673) + ($100,000 × 0.840) = $10,692 + $84,000 = $94,692。表面利率（4%）が市場利率（6%）を下回るため、債券は額面未満のディスカウント価格で取引される。$84,000は元本の現在価値のみで利息を omit している。$100,000は額面金額であり、表面利率と市場利率が等しい場合にのみ価格となる。$96,000は元本の現在価値に割引前の利息総額（$12,000）を加えたもので、クーポンの割引計算を行っていない。

questions.subtopic: Equity Securities - Stock Dividend and Subsequent Sale
questions.stem: In January Year 1, Calder Corp. purchased 10,000 shares of Voss Inc. common stock (less than a 5% interest, with a readily determinable fair value) for $120,000. In March Year 1, Calder received a 25% stock dividend from Voss. In June Year 1, before any fair value remeasurement date, Calder sold 2,000 of its Voss shares for $11 per share. What amount of gain or loss should Calder recognize on the sale?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $2,800

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $2,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $22,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $0

questions.explanation: A stock dividend is not income to the holder; instead, the original cost is spread over the larger number of shares. After the 25% stock dividend, Calder holds 10,000 × 1.25 = 12,500 shares with the same total cost of $120,000, so the cost per share becomes $120,000 ÷ 12,500 = $9.60. The gain on selling 2,000 shares at $11 = 2,000 × ($11.00 − $9.60) = $2,800. A loss of $2,000 results from incorrectly using the original $12 per-share cost (2,000 × ($11 − $12)), which fails to reallocate cost over the new shares. A gain of $22,000 results from incorrectly assigning a zero basis to shares received as a stock dividend. A gain of $0 would be wrong because the sale price differs from the adjusted per-share cost.
questions.explanationJa: 株式配当は受領者の収益ではなく、取得原価をより多い株式数に配分し直す。25%の株式配当後、Calderの保有株式数は10,000 × 1.25 = 12,500株となり、取得原価総額$120,000は変わらないため、1株当たり原価は$120,000 ÷ 12,500 = $9.60となる。2,000株を$11で売却した利得 = 2,000 × ($11.00 − $9.60) = $2,800。$2,000の損失は当初の1株$12の原価をそのまま使用した誤り（2,000 × ($11 − $12)）で、新株への原価再配分を行っていない。$22,000の利得は株式配当で受け取った株式の原価をゼロとみなす誤りによる。売却価格は修正後の1株当たり原価と異なるため、利得ゼロも誤りである。

questions.stem: Under ASC 825, at which of the following times may an entity elect the fair value option for an eligible financial instrument?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At any reporting date, provided the election is disclosed in the notes

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only at the end of the fiscal year in which the instrument was acquired

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the instrument is first recognized, or upon a qualifying event such as an investment becoming subject to the equity method

choices.label: D
choices.text: Whenever the instrument's fair value begins to diverge significantly from its carrying amount

questions.explanation: The fair value option may be elected only on specified election dates: generally when the entity first recognizes the eligible item, or upon certain qualifying events—for example, when an investment becomes subject to the equity method, when an entity enters into a firm commitment, or upon a business combination. Electing at any reporting date with disclosure would allow opportunistic earnings management and is prohibited. There is no rule limiting the election to fiscal year-end. Divergence between fair value and carrying amount is not an election trigger; allowing elections based on price movements is precisely what the specified-date requirement prevents.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値オプションは特定の選択日（election dates）にのみ選択できる。原則として対象項目の当初認識時、または一定の適格事象が生じた時（例：投資が持分法の適用対象となった時、確定約定を締結した時、企業結合時など）である。任意の報告日に注記開示を条件に選択できるとすれば恣意的な利益操作を許すことになり、禁止されている。選択を会計年度末に限定する規定はない。公正価値と帳簿価額の乖離は選択の契機ではなく、価格変動に応じた選択を防ぐことこそが特定選択日要件の趣旨である。

questions.subtopic: CECL - Zero Expected Credit Loss Securities
questions.stem: Harlow Corp. holds U.S. Treasury securities classified as held-to-maturity. Under ASC 326, how should Harlow apply the current expected credit loss (CECL) model to these securities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record an allowance equal to the difference between amortized cost and fair value whenever fair value is lower

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record an allowance based on the historical default rate of all corporate bonds

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a minimum allowance of 1% of amortized cost as a regulatory floor

choices.label: D
choices.text: Harlow may conclude that the expectation of nonpayment is zero and record no allowance for credit losses

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20 permits an entity to conclude that the expected credit loss on a financial asset is zero when the expectation of nonpayment of the amortized cost basis is zero—U.S. Treasury securities, which are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. government, are the classic example. In that case no allowance is recorded. Measuring an allowance as the shortfall of fair value below amortized cost confuses the CECL model with fair value impairment; interest-rate-driven fair value declines are not credit losses. Using corporate bond default rates ignores that the estimate must reflect the specific asset's credit characteristics. There is no 1% minimum allowance floor in ASC 326.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20は、償却原価の不払いの予想がゼロである場合、予想信用損失をゼロと結論づけることを認めている。米国政府の信用力に裏付けられた米国財務省証券（U.S. Treasury）はその典型例であり、この場合、信用損失引当金は計上しない。公正価値が償却原価を下回る額を引当金とする処理はCECLモデルと公正価値減損の混同であり、金利変動による公正価値の下落は信用損失ではない。社債のデフォルト率を使う方法は、見積りが当該資産固有の信用特性を反映すべきであることを無視している。ASC 326に償却原価の1%といった最低引当率の規定は存在しない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 321, how is the election to use the measurement alternative for equity securities without readily determinable fair values made?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Separately for each investment, and the entity must reassess at each reporting period whether the security continues to qualify

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a single accounting policy election that applies to all equity securities held by the entity

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only with the approval of the entity's external auditor

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only for investments representing more than 20% of the investee's voting stock

questions.explanation: The measurement alternative is elected on an investment-by-investment basis, not as an entity-wide policy, and the entity must reassess at each reporting period whether the security still lacks a readily determinable fair value and remains outside the NAV practical expedient. If fair value becomes readily determinable, the measurement alternative must be discontinued and the security measured at fair value through net income. An entity-wide policy election mischaracterizes the unit of account for the election. Auditor approval is not an accounting requirement. Ownership above 20% with significant influence would generally point to the equity method, which is outside the scope of ASC 321 entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 測定代替（measurement alternative）の選択は企業全体の会計方針としてではなく、投資ごと（investment-by-investment）に行う。また、各報告期間において、当該証券が引き続き容易に算定可能な公正価値を欠いており、NAV実務上の便法の対象外であるかを再評価しなければならない。公正価値が容易に算定可能になった場合は測定代替を中止し、公正価値（評価差額は純利益）で測定する。企業全体での一括選択は選択の単位を誤っている。外部監査人の承認は会計上の要件ではない。議決権の20%超を保有し重要な影響力がある場合は通常持分法が適用され、そもそもASC 321の適用範囲外となる。

questions.subtopic: HTM - Sale After Substantial Principal Collection
questions.stem: Mercer Corp. sells a held-to-maturity debt security after having already collected 85% of the principal outstanding at acquisition through scheduled payments on the amortizing security. Does this sale call into question Mercer's intent to hold other debt securities to maturity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes; any sale of a held-to-maturity security taints the entire held-to-maturity portfolio

choices.label: B
choices.text: No; a sale after collection of a substantial portion (at least 85%) of the principal is treated as equivalent to holding the security to maturity

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, unless the security is replaced with another security of equal face value within 30 days

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, provided the sale proceeds are reinvested in held-to-maturity securities

questions.explanation: ASC 320 treats certain sales of held-to-maturity securities as equivalent to holding them to maturity. One safe harbor applies when the sale occurs after the entity has already collected a substantial portion—at least 85%—of the principal outstanding at acquisition. Such a sale does not taint the remaining held-to-maturity portfolio. The statement that any sale automatically taints the portfolio is too broad, because the standard provides specific safe harbors (including sales near maturity and sales after substantial principal collection). There is no replacement-security cure provision, and reinvesting the proceeds in other held-to-maturity securities does not by itself prevent tainting from an otherwise inconsistent sale.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320は、満期保有証券の一定の売却を満期まで保有した場合と同等として扱う。セーフハーバーの一つは、取得時の元本残高の相当部分（85%以上）を既に回収した後の売却であり、この場合、残りの満期保有ポートフォリオは「汚染（tainting）」されない。あらゆる売却が自動的にポートフォリオを汚染するという記述は広すぎる。基準は特定のセーフハーバー（満期間際の売却、元本の相当部分回収後の売却など）を定めているからである。代替証券を購入すれば治癒されるという規定は存在せず、売却代金を他の満期保有証券に再投資すること自体も、本来不整合な売却による汚染を防ぐものではない。

questions.stem: Tilden Corp. paid an amount for a 30% equity method investment that exceeds its share of the fair value of the investee's identifiable net assets, creating equity method goodwill. How should this equity method goodwill be accounted for after acquisition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Amortized on a straight-line basis over 10 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized as a separate goodwill asset and tested annually for impairment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included in the carrying amount of the investment and not amortized; the investment as a whole is evaluated for impairment

choices.label: D
choices.text: Charged immediately to expense in the period of acquisition

questions.explanation: Equity method goodwill remains embedded in the carrying amount of the investment. It is neither amortized nor tested separately for impairment; instead, the investment in its entirety is evaluated for an other-than-temporary decline in value under ASC 323. Straight-line amortization over 10 years describes the private-company accounting alternative for goodwill recognized in a business combination, not equity method goodwill. Recognizing a separate goodwill asset with annual impairment testing applies to goodwill arising in consolidation under ASC 350, not to an equity method investment. Immediate expensing has no basis; the excess payment is part of the cost of the investment.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法のれん（equity method goodwill）は投資勘定の帳簿価額に含まれたままとなる。償却もされず、のれん単独での減損テストも行わない。代わりに、ASC 323の下で投資全体について一時的でない価値の下落（other-than-temporary decline）の有無を評価する。10年間の定額償却は企業結合で認識されたのれんに対する非公開会社向けの会計上の代替処理であり、持分法のれんには適用されない。独立したのれん資産として計上し毎年減損テストを行うのはASC 350に基づく連結上ののれんの処理である。即時費用化に根拠はなく、超過支払額は投資原価の一部である。

questions.subtopic: Measurement Alternative - Observable Increase Followed by Impairment
questions.stem: Pemberton Corp. holds equity securities of a private company without a readily determinable fair value and applies the measurement alternative. The carrying amount on January 1, Year 1 is $250,000 (original cost). In March Year 1, an orderly observable transaction in an identical security of the same issuer indicates a value of $290,000 for Pemberton's holding, and Pemberton adjusts the carrying amount. In December Year 1, a qualitative assessment indicates the investment is impaired, and Pemberton estimates its fair value at $230,000. What is the net effect of these events on Pemberton's Year 1 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $60,000 net decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $40,000 net increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect; both adjustments are recorded in other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: $20,000 net decrease

questions.explanation: Under the measurement alternative, the carrying amount is adjusted upward or downward for observable price changes in orderly transactions for identical or similar investments of the same issuer, with the adjustment recognized in net income. The March observable transaction increases the carrying amount from $250,000 to $290,000, producing a $40,000 gain in net income. When a qualitative impairment assessment indicates impairment, the security is written down to fair value through net income: $290,000 − $230,000 = $60,000 impairment loss. Net effect on Year 1 net income = $40,000 gain − $60,000 loss = $20,000 net decrease. A $60,000 decrease counts only the impairment and ignores the earlier upward adjustment. A $40,000 increase ignores the impairment. Recording the adjustments in other comprehensive income is wrong because all measurement alternative adjustments flow through net income.
questions.explanationJa: 測定代替の下では、同一発行体の同一または類似の投資に関する秩序ある取引の観察可能な価格変動に応じて帳簿価額を増減させ、その調整額は純利益に認識する。3月の観察可能な取引により帳簿価額は$250,000から$290,000に増加し、$40,000の利得が純利益に計上される。定性的評価で減損が示された場合は公正価値まで切り下げ、純利益に損失を計上する：$290,000 − $230,000 = $60,000の減損損失。Year 1の純利益への正味の影響 = 利得$40,000 − 損失$60,000 = $20,000の純減少。$60,000の減少は減損のみを数え、先行する上方調整を無視している。$40,000の増加は減損を無視している。その他の包括利益への計上は誤りで、測定代替に基づく調整はすべて純利益を通して認識される。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Downstream Sale with Partial Resale
questions.stem: Ashford Corp. owns 30% of Birchwood Inc. and uses the equity method. During Year 1, Ashford sold inventory to Birchwood at a profit of $80,000 (a downstream sale). By December 31, Year 1, Birchwood had resold 75% of that inventory to unrelated third parties. By what amount should Ashford reduce its equity in earnings of Birchwood for Year 1 because of the intra-entity profit?
questions.explanation: Only the profit on inventory still held by the investee at year-end is unrealized. Unrealized profit = $80,000 × 25% remaining = $20,000. Under the equity method, the investor eliminates its proportionate share of the intra-entity profit: $20,000 × 30% = $6,000 reduction in equity in earnings. The $20,000 amount is the full unrealized profit without applying the investor's 30% ownership percentage. The $24,000 amount applies the 30% to the entire $80,000 profit, ignoring that 75% of the inventory was already resold and that profit is therefore realized. Eliminating nothing would overstate earnings, because the profit on the 25% still on hand has not been confirmed by a sale to outside parties.
questions.explanationJa: 未実現となるのは、期末時点で被投資会社が保有し続けている在庫に係る利益のみである。未実現利益 = $80,000 × 残存25% = $20,000。持分法では投資会社は企業グループ内利益のうち持分相当額を消去する：$20,000 × 30% = $6,000を持分法投資利益から減額する。$20,000は投資会社の持分割合30%を適用していない未実現利益の全額である。$24,000は利益$80,000全額に30%を乗じたもので、在庫の75%が既に外部へ再販売され、その分の利益が実現済みであることを無視している。何も消去しない処理は、手元に残る25%分の利益が外部への販売によって確定していないため、利益の過大計上となる。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Mid-Year Acquisition with Basis Difference
questions.stem: On July 1, Year 1, Granger Corp. acquired 40% of Holt Inc. for $500,000, obtaining significant influence. At that date, the book value of Holt's net assets was $1,000,000, and the entire excess of cost over Granger's share of book value was attributable to equipment with a remaining useful life of 5 years (straight-line). Holt reported net income of $200,000 for Year 1, earned evenly throughout the year, and declared no dividends. What amount should Granger report as equity in earnings of Holt for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Granger's share of book value = $1,000,000 × 40% = $400,000, so the excess of cost over book value = $500,000 − $400,000 = $100,000, all attributable to equipment. Annual excess depreciation = $100,000 ÷ 5 = $20,000. Because the investment was held for only half of Year 1, equity in earnings = (40% × $200,000 × 6/12) − ($20,000 × 6/12) = $40,000 − $10,000 = $30,000. The $40,000 amount omits the basis difference amortization. The $60,000 amount uses a full year of income share and amortization ($80,000 − $20,000), ignoring the July 1 acquisition date. The $80,000 amount is a full-year income share with no amortization at all.
questions.explanationJa: Grangerの簿価持分 = $1,000,000 × 40% = $400,000。取得原価の簿価超過額 = $500,000 − $400,000 = $100,000で、全額が設備に帰属する。超過減価償却の年額 = $100,000 ÷ 5 = $20,000。投資はYear 1の後半6か月のみ保有しているため、持分法投資利益 = (40% × $200,000 × 6/12) − ($20,000 × 6/12) = $40,000 − $10,000 = $30,000。$40,000は基礎差額の償却を omit している。$60,000は利益持分と償却を1年分（$80,000 − $20,000）で計算し、7月1日の取得日を無視している。$80,000は償却を全く行わない1年分の利益持分である。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Cash Flow Classification of Dividends Received
questions.stem: Selby Corp. uses the equity method for its 30% investment in Thorne Inc. During Year 1, Selby received cash dividends from Thorne that did not exceed Selby's cumulative equity in Thorne's earnings since the investment was acquired. Under the cumulative earnings approach in ASC 230, how should Selby classify the dividends received in its statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a financing activity, because dividends relate to equity interests

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a noncash transaction disclosed in a supplemental schedule

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an investing activity, because the dividends reduce the investment account

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an operating activity, because the distribution is a return on investment

questions.explanation: Under the cumulative earnings approach, distributions received from an equity method investee are presumed to be returns on investment—classified as operating cash inflows—to the extent they do not exceed the investor's cumulative equity in the investee's earnings. Only the excess over cumulative earnings is treated as a return of investment and classified as an investing inflow. Because Selby's dividends did not exceed cumulative equity in earnings, the entire amount is an operating inflow. Financing classification applies to transactions with the entity's own capital providers, such as dividends the entity pays, not dividends it receives. The dividends were received in cash, so noncash disclosure does not apply. Investing classification would be correct only for the portion exceeding cumulative equity in earnings, which does not exist here.
questions.explanationJa: 累積利益アプローチ（cumulative earnings approach）では、持分法適用会社から受け取った分配は、投資会社の累積持分法利益を超えない範囲で「投資に対するリターン（return on investment）」と推定され、営業活動によるキャッシュ・インフローに分類される。累積利益を超える部分のみが「投資の回収（return of investment）」として投資活動に分類される。Selbyの受取配当は累積持分法利益を超えていないため、全額が営業活動のインフローとなる。財務活動は自社の資本提供者との取引（自社が支払う配当など）に適用され、受取配当には当てはまらない。配当は現金で受領しているため非現金取引の補足開示は該当しない。投資活動への分類は累積利益超過部分にのみ正しく、本問にはその部分が存在しない。

questions.subtopic: Equity Method - Gain on Sale of Entire Investment
questions.stem: Norwood Corp. owns 25% of Pratt Inc. and uses the equity method. The investment's carrying amount at January 1, Year 1 was $560,000. Pratt reported net income of $80,000 for the first quarter of Year 1 (January 1 through March 31), and Norwood received a $5,000 cash dividend from Pratt in February. On April 1, Year 1, Norwood sold its entire interest in Pratt for $610,000. What gain should Norwood recognize on the sale?
questions.explanation: The investment account must be updated through the date of sale before computing the gain. Equity in earnings for the first quarter = $80,000 × 25% = $20,000 (increase). Dividend received = $5,000 (decrease). Carrying amount at April 1 = $560,000 + $20,000 − $5,000 = $575,000. Gain on sale = $610,000 − $575,000 = $35,000. The $50,000 amount compares the proceeds with the unadjusted January 1 balance of $560,000. The $30,000 amount adds the earnings share but fails to subtract the dividend ($610,000 − $580,000). The $55,000 amount incorrectly adds the dividend back to the beginning balance instead of subtracting it.
questions.explanationJa: 売却損益の計算前に、投資勘定を売却日まで更新しなければならない。第1四半期の持分法投資利益 = $80,000 × 25% = $20,000（増加）。受取配当 = $5,000（減少）。4月1日の帳簿価額 = $560,000 + $20,000 − $5,000 = $575,000。売却益 = $610,000 − $575,000 = $35,000。$50,000は売却代金を未調整の1月1日残高$560,000と比較したものである。$30,000は利益持分を加算する一方で配当の控除を行っていない（$610,000 − $580,000）。$55,000は配当を期首残高から控除する代わりに誤って加算したものである。

questions.stem: What effect would the sale of a company's trading debt securities at their carrying amounts for cash have on each of the following ratios? Current ratio Quick ratio

choices.label: A
choices.text: No effect No effect

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase Increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect Increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase No effect

questions.explanation: Trading debt securities are classified as current assets and are included in both the current ratio (current assets / current liabilities) and the quick ratio (cash + short-term investments + net receivables / current liabilities). When trading securities are sold at carrying value for cash, one current asset (trading securities) is replaced by another current asset of equal value (cash). Both the current ratio and the quick ratio numerator remain unchanged, so neither ratio is affected. There is no effect on current liabilities either.
questions.explanationJa: 売買目的有価証券は流動資産に分類され、流動比率（流動資産÷流動負債）および当座比率（当座資産÷流動負債）の分子に含まれます。売買目的有価証券を帳簿価額で現金と交換した場合、流動資産内で売買目的有価証券（流動資産）が現金（流動資産）に置き換わるだけで、流動資産合計も流動負債も変化しません。したがって両方の比率に影響はありません。

questions.stem: On June 30, Trey Co. purchased a bond, with a face value of $200,000 and a stated interest rate of 5%, at 98% of face value. Interest is payable on June 30 and December 31. The company intends to hold the investment until maturity and does not elect the fair value option. What is the initial carrying value on June 30?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 320 for held-to-maturity (HTM) debt securities, the investment is initially recorded at cost (purchase price). Purchase price = $200,000 × 98% = $196,000. This is the initial carrying value (amortized cost basis at acquisition). The discount of $4,000 ($200,000 − $196,000) will be amortized over the life of the bond using the effective interest method, but the initial carrying value is simply $196,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320により、満期保有目的有価証券は取得原価（取得価格）で当初計上する。取得価格 = $200,000 × 98% = $196,000。これが取得日の帳簿価額（償却原価）となる。割引額$4,000（= $200,000 − $196,000）は実効利子率法により満期まで償却する。

questions.stem: Plack Co. purchased 10,000 shares (2% ownership) of Ty Corp. on February 14, Year 1. Plack received a stock dividend of 2,000 shares on April 30, Year 1, when the market value per share was $35. Ty paid a cash dividend of $2 per share on December 15, Year 1. In its Year 1 income statement, what amount should Plack report as dividend income?
questions.explanation: Stock dividends are not recognized as income — they merely represent an increase in the number of shares held with a corresponding decrease in the per-share cost basis (no cash received, no gain). Only cash dividends are recognized as dividend income. After the stock dividend, Plack holds 10,000 + 2,000 = 12,000 shares. Cash dividend income = 12,000 shares × $2 = $24,000. The stock dividend fair value of $70,000 (2,000 × $35) is not income.
questions.explanationJa: 株式配当は収益として認識しない（受取株数が増加し1株当たりコスト基準が減少するのみ）。配当収入として認識されるのは現金配当のみ。株式配当後の保有株数 = 10,000 + 2,000 = 12,000株。現金配当収入 = 12,000株 × $2 = $24,000。株式配当の公正価値$70,000は収益に計上しない。

questions.stem: Which of the following items is required to be reported at fair value in a company's financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Bonds payable.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Financial futures contracts.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Written loan commitments.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Trade receivables.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815 and ASC 820, derivative instruments including financial futures contracts are required to be measured and reported at fair value on the balance sheet. Bonds payable are carried at amortized cost (unless the fair value option is elected). Trade receivables are measured at net realizable value (amortized cost net of allowance for credit losses). Written loan commitments may qualify as derivatives in certain circumstances but are not universally required to be at fair value. Financial futures contracts are derivatives and are mandatorily measured at fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 815およびASC 820の下で、金融先物契約などのデリバティブ商品はバランスシートで公正価値で強制的に測定・表示される。社債は（公正価値オプションを選択しない限り）償却原価で計上される。売掛金は純実現可能価額（貸倒引当金控除後の償却原価）で計上される。金融先物契約はデリバティブであり公正価値での計上が必須。

questions.stem:
The following information pertains to Lark Corp.'s long-term marketable debt securities portfolio classified as available for sale:

| | 12/31/Year 2 | 12/31/Year 1 |
|---|---|---|
| Cost | $200,000 | $200,000 |
| Fair value | 240,000 | 180,000 |

Differences between cost and fair values are considered temporary and noncredit related. The decline from cost to fair value was properly accounted for at December 31, Year 1. By what amount should the valuation accounts related to the marketable debt securities change from December 31, Year 1, to December 31, Year 2?
questions.explanation: For available-for-sale debt securities, the unrealized holding gain or loss (noncredit related) is recorded in accumulated other comprehensive income (AOCI) and a corresponding valuation allowance adjusts the carrying value. At December 31, Year 1: unrealized loss = $200,000 − $180,000 = $20,000 (AOCI debit / valuation contra-asset). At December 31, Year 2: unrealized gain = $240,000 − $200,000 = $40,000 (AOCI credit / valuation asset). The change in the valuation account from −$20,000 to +$40,000 = a change of $60,000.
questions.explanationJa: 売却可能有価証券（非信用関連）の未実現保有損益はAOCIおよび評価勘定に反映される。Year 1末：未実現損失 = $200,000 − $180,000 = −$20,000（評価勘定は−$20,000）。Year 2末：未実現利益 = $240,000 − $200,000 = +$40,000（評価勘定は+$40,000）。評価勘定の変化額 = −$20,000 → +$40,000 = $60,000の変動。

questions.stem: Park Co. uses the equity method to account for its January 1, Year 3 purchase of Tun Inc.'s common stock. On January 1, Year 3, the fair values of Tun's FIFO inventory and land exceeded their carrying amounts. How do these excesses of fair values over carrying amounts affect Park's reported equity in Tun's Year 3 earnings? Inventory Excess Land Excess

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease Decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease No effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase Increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase No effect

questions.explanation: Under the equity method (ASC 323), when the purchase price exceeds the book value of the investee's net assets, the investor must amortize the excess fair-value-over-book-value allocations against its equity in investee earnings. The inventory excess is amortized when the inventory is sold (Year 3, assuming FIFO moves the beginning inventory first), which decreases Park's equity in Tun's earnings. Land is not depreciated or amortized because it has an indefinite useful life, so the land excess has no effect on Park's equity in Tun's Year 3 earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法（ASC 323）において、取得価額が被投資会社純資産帳簿価額を超える場合、投資会社はその超過額（公正価値と帳簿価額の差額）を被投資会社の収益の持分取り込み額から償却する。棚卸資産（FIFO）の超過額は棚卸資産が売却された時点で償却されるため、Parkの持分取り込み額を減少させる。土地は耐用年数が無限であるため償却・減価償却されず、土地超過額はYear 3の持分取り込み額に影響を与えない。

questions.stem: Reed, Inc. began operations on January 1, Year 1. The following information pertains to Reed's December 31, Year 1, portfolio of marketable debt securities: Trading Available-for-sale Aggregate cost $360,000 $550,000 Aggregate fair value 320,000 450,000 Aggregate lower of cost or fair value applied to each security in the portfolio 304,000 420,000 If the fair value declines are judged to be temporary and noncredit related, what amounts should Reed report for its trading securities and available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities in its December 31, Year 1, balance sheet? Trading securities AFS debt securities
questions.explanation: Under ASC 320, both trading securities and available-for-sale debt securities are reported at fair value on the balance sheet — the lower-of-cost-or-market rule does not apply. Trading securities are reported at aggregate fair value = $320,000, with unrealized losses ($40,000) recorded in net income. Available-for-sale debt securities are reported at aggregate fair value = $450,000, with the noncredit unrealized loss ($100,000) recorded in accumulated other comprehensive income (AOCI). The 'lower of cost or fair value applied to each security' ($304,000 and $420,000) is not the correct carrying value for either category.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320により、売買目的有価証券・売却可能有価証券のいずれも公正価値でバランスシートに計上する。売買目的有価証券：公正価値$320,000（未実現損失$40,000は損益計上）。売却可能有価証券：公正価値$450,000（非信用関連未実現損失$100,000はAOCIに計上）。個別低価法（$304,000・$420,000）はどちらの分類にも適用されない。

questions.stem: During Year 3, Gilman Co. purchased 5,000 shares (1% ownership) of Meteor Corp.'s common stock for $35,000. During Year 3, Gilman received $1,800 of dividends from its investment in Meteor's stock. The fair value of Gilman's investment on December 31, Year 3, is $32,000. What amount of income or loss that is attributable to the Meteor stock investment should be reflected in Gilman's earnings for Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Income of $1,800.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Income of $4,800.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $1,200.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $3,000.

questions.explanation: Gilman owns less than 20% of Meteor's stock and does not exercise significant influence, so this is an equity security without a readily determinable fair value — however, per ASC 321, equity securities with a readily determinable fair value are measured at fair value through net income. Under ASC 321, unrealized holding gains and losses on equity securities are recognized in earnings. Unrealized loss = Fair value $32,000 − Cost $35,000 = −$3,000. Dividend income = $1,800. Net income impact = $1,800 − $3,000 = −$1,200 (net loss of $1,200).
questions.explanationJa: Gilmanは持分が1%（重要な影響力なし）のため、ASC 321の持分有価証券として公正価値で損益計上する。未実現損失 = 公正価値$32,000 − 取得原価$35,000 = −$3,000。配当収入 = $1,800。純損益への影響 = $1,800 − $3,000 = −$1,200（純損失$1,200）。

questions.stem: During the current year, Cooley Co. had an unrealized gain (related to changes in market risk) of $100,000 on a debt investment classified as available-for-sale. Cooley's corporate tax rate is 25%. What amount of the gain should be included in Cooley's net income and other comprehensive income at the end of the current year? Net Income Other Comprehensive Income
questions.explanation: Under ASC 320, unrealized holding gains and losses on available-for-sale (AFS) debt securities that are noncredit related (arising from changes in market risk) are excluded from net income and instead reported in other comprehensive income (OCI), net of tax. The pretax unrealized gain = $100,000. The tax effect = $100,000 × 25% = $25,000. The after-tax unrealized gain recognized in OCI = $100,000 − $25,000 = $75,000. Net income = $0 from this gain (it does not pass through the income statement).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320により、売却可能有価証券（AFS）の市場リスクに起因する非信用関連の未実現保有利益は、純利益に含めずその他包括利益（OCI）に税効果控除後で計上する。税引前未実現利益$100,000 × (1 − 25%) = $75,000がOCIに計上される。純利益への影響はゼロ。

questions.stem: Anchor Co. owns 40% of Main Co.'s common stock outstanding and 75% of Main's noncumulative preferred stock outstanding. Anchor exercises significant influence over Main's operations. During the current period, Main declared dividends of $200,000 on its common stock and $100,000 on its noncumulative preferred stock. What amount of dividend income should Anchor report on its income statement for the current period related to its investment in Main?
questions.explanation: Anchor exercises significant influence over Main's common stock (40% ownership), so the equity method applies to the common stock investment — under the equity method, dividends received from common stock reduce the investment carrying value and are not reported as dividend income. For the preferred stock, Anchor owns 75% of noncumulative preferred and does not exercise significant influence through preferred stock; preferred dividends are reported as dividend income. Anchor's preferred dividend income = 75% × $100,000 = $75,000.
questions.explanationJa: Anchorは普通株式を40%保有し重要な影響力を行使するため、普通株式への投資は持分法を適用する。持分法の下では受取配当金（普通株）は配当収入ではなく投資の帳簿価額の減少として処理される。優先株式については持分法の適用対象外であり、受取配当金は配当収入として報告する。優先配当収入 = 75% × $100,000 = $75,000。

questions.stem: Larkin Co. has owned 25% of the common stock of Devon Co. for a number of years and has the ability to exercise significant influence over Devon. The following information relates to Larkin's investment in Devon during the most recent year: Carrying amount of Larkin's investment in Devon at the beginning of the year $200,000 Net income of Devon for the year 600,000 Total dividends paid to Devon's stockholders during the year 400,000 What is the carrying amount of Larkin's investment in Devon at year end?
questions.explanation: Under the equity method (ASC 323), the investor's carrying amount is adjusted each period: beginning balance + proportionate share of net income − proportionate share of dividends. Calculation: $200,000 + (25% × $600,000) − (25% × $400,000) = $200,000 + $150,000 − $100,000 = $250,000.

questions.stem: On January 1 of the current year, Barton Co. paid $900,000 for a two-year, 8%, $1,000,000 face value bonds that were issued by another publicly traded corporation. Barton plans to sell the bonds in the first quarter of the following year. The fair value of the bonds at the end of the current year was $1,020,000. At what amount should Barton report the bonds in its balance sheet at the end of the current year?
questions.explanation: Because Barton intends to sell the bonds within the next year, they are classified as trading securities under ASC 320. Trading securities are carried at fair value with changes reported in net income. Therefore the bonds are reported at their year-end fair value of $1,020,000.
questions.explanationJa: 翌年前半に売却予定のため、ASC 320に従いトレーディング証券に分類されます。トレーディング証券は公正価値で評価し、変動は損益に計上するため、期末公正価値$1,020,000で報告します。

questions.stem: Birk Co. purchased 30% of Sled Co.'s outstanding common stock on December 31, Year 1, for $200,000. On that date, Sled's stockholders' equity was $500,000, and the fair value of its identifiable net assets was $600,000. On December 31, Year 1, what amount of goodwill should Birk attribute to this acquisition?
questions.explanation: Under the equity method (ASC 323), implicit goodwill is the excess of purchase price over the investor's share of the fair value of identifiable net assets. Birk's share of fair value of identifiable net assets = 30% × $600,000 = $180,000. Goodwill = $200,000 − $180,000 = $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法（ASC 323）では、購入対価から識別可能純資産の公正価値の持分割合を差し引いたものがのれんとなります。純資産公正価値の持分分=30%×$600,000=$180,000。のれん=$200,000−$180,000=$20,000。

questions.stem: A company has a 22% investment in another company that it accounts for using the equity method. Which of the following disclosures should be included in the company's annual financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The names and ownership percentages of the other stockholders in the investee company.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The reason for the company's decision to invest in the investee company.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The company's accounting policy for the investment.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Whether the investee company is involved in any litigation.

questions.explanation: ASC 323-10-50 requires disclosure of the accounting policy used for the equity-method investment. The other options — names of other investors, strategic rationale for investing, or the investee's litigation — are not required disclosures under GAAP for equity-method investments.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323-10-50では、持分法投資に適用した会計方針の開示が求められます。他の株主名、投資理由、被投資会社の訴訟情報は開示必須事項ではありません。

questions.stem: Jenler Co. uses the equity method to account for its January 1, Year 4, purchase of Raston Inc.'s common stock. On January 1, Year 4, the fair values of Raston's FIFO inventory and equipment exceeded their carrying amounts. How do these excesses of fair values over carrying amounts affect Jenler's reported equity in Raston's Year 4 earnings? Inventory excess Equipment excess

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease Decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease No effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase Increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase No effect

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323, the investor must amortize the excess of fair value over book value of the investee's assets into its equity-method earnings. For FIFO inventory, the excess fair value is charged to cost of goods sold when inventory is sold (reducing income). For equipment, the excess is depreciated over the asset's remaining life (reducing income). Both adjustments decrease Jenler's reported equity in Raston's earnings.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323に従い、持分法では被投資会社資産の公正価値超過額を投資家の持分利益調整として認識します。FIFO棚卸資産の超過額は売上原価に算入（利益減少）、設備の超過額は残存耐用年数にわたり減価償却（利益減少）されます。どちらもJenlerの持分利益を減少させます。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Peabody Co. purchased an investment for $400,000 that represented 30% of Newman Corp.'s outstanding voting stock. For Year 1, Newman reported net income of $60,000 and paid dividends of $20,000. At year end, the fair value of Peabody's investment in Newman was $410,000. Peabody elected the fair value option for this investment. What amount should Peabody recognize in net income for Year 1 attributable to the investment?
questions.explanation: When the fair value option (ASC 825) is elected for an equity-method investment, the investor reports changes in fair value through net income. Under the fair value option, dividends received are also recognized in net income. The unrealized gain = $410,000 − $400,000 = $10,000. Dividends received = 30% × $20,000 = $6,000. However, when the fair value option is elected, dividends are simply treated as dividend income recognized separately, while the fair value change ($10,000 gain) is recognized in earnings. Total net income = $10,000 fair value change + $6,000 dividends? No — under ASC 825 fair value option the carrying amount is adjusted to fair value each period; dividends received are not separately recognized as income because they reduce the fair value. The gain recognized = ending fair value $410,000 − beginning fair value $400,000 = $10,000.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値オプション（ASC 825）を選択した場合、投資の公正価値変動は損益として認識します。期末公正価値$410,000−取得原価$400,000＝$10,000の未実現利益が当期純利益に含まれます。配当は公正価値に織り込まれているため別途認識しません。

questions.stem: Data regarding an entity's available-for-sale debt securities follow: Cost Allowance for credit losses Market value December 31, Year 3 $200,000 $5,000 $185,000 December 31, Year 4 $200,000 $2,000 $195,000 Differences between cost and market value are considered temporary. The entity does not elect the fair value option to account for available-for-sale securities. The entity's Year 4 other comprehensive income would be
questions.explanation: For AFS debt securities, the noncredit component of unrealized gains/losses flows through OCI (ASC 320). At Year 3, the noncredit unrealized loss = market value $185,000 − (cost $200,000 − credit allowance $5,000) = $185,000 − $195,000 = −$10,000 in OCI. At Year 4, the noncredit unrealized loss = $195,000 − ($200,000 − $2,000) = $195,000 − $198,000 = −$3,000 in OCI. The change in OCI from Year 3 to Year 4 = −$3,000 − (−$10,000) = +$7,000, which is reported as Year 4 OCI income.
questions.explanationJa: AFS債務証券の非信用リスク部分の未実現損益はOCI（ASC 320）を通じて処理します。Year 3のOCIポジション：$185,000−($200,000−$5,000)＝−$10,000。Year 4のOCIポジション：$195,000−($200,000−$2,000)＝−$3,000。OCI変動＝−$3,000−(−$10,000)＝+$7,000がYear 4のOCI収益となります。

questions.stem: On December 31, Year 3, Ott Co. had investments in marketable equity securities as follows: Cost Fair value Man Co. $10,000 $8,000 Kemo, Inc. 9,000 11,000 Fenn Corp. 11,000 9,000 Ownership in each security represents less than 20% of the outstanding stock. What amounts, related to these investments, would Ott report on its year-end balance sheet and income statement? Balance sheet Income statement

choices.label: A
choices.text: $28,000 $2,000 realized loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: $28,000 $2,000 unrealized loss

choices.label: C
choices.text: $30,000 $2,000 realized loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: $30,000 $0 gain or loss

questions.explanation: Marketable equity securities without significant influence are measured at fair value with changes in fair value reported in net income (ASC 321). Total fair value = $8,000 + $11,000 + $9,000 = $28,000, which is reported on the balance sheet. Net unrealized gain/loss = $28,000 − $30,000 cost = −$2,000, reported as an unrealized loss in the income statement.
questions.explanationJa: 重要な影響力のない上場株式はASC 321に従い公正価値で測定し、変動を損益計上します。B/S計上額：$8,000+$11,000+$9,000＝$28,000。未実現損失：$28,000−$30,000＝−$2,000を損益計算書で認識します。

questions.stem: At the end of Year 3, Shore Co. held trading securities that cost $17,500 and had a year-end market value of $19,000. All these securities were sold at the beginning of Year 4 for $22,000. During Year 4, Shore transferred securities classified as available-for-sale to trading securities. At the time of the transfer, these securities had a fair value of $30,000 and an unrealized holding gain of $4,000. For the year ended on December 31, Year 4, what is the impact of these transactions on Shore's income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $3,000.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $4,000.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $4,500.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $7,000.

questions.explanation: Two income statement effects in Year 4: (1) Sale of former trading securities: proceeds $22,000 − Year 3 carrying value $19,000 = $3,000 realized gain. (2) Transfer of AFS to trading: at transfer date the unrealized gain of $4,000 previously in AOCI is reclassified and recognized in net income, and the security is now carried at fair value $30,000. However, on the same day of transfer these newly reclassified securities are already at fair value, so no additional gain on the transfer day itself. Net income impact = $3,000 realized gain on sale. The $4,000 AOCI reclassification is also recognized in income, but the question focuses only on the trading security sale — total = $3,000 gain. Actually re-reading: the $4,000 unrealized holding gain in AOCI IS reclassified to income upon transfer (ASC 320-10-35-10). But the sale gain from ex-trading = $22,000 − $19,000 = $3,000. Total = $3,000 + $0 (no additional change at transfer day) = $3,000. The $4,000 gain was already in AOCI and is now in income, netting against the transfer: $3,000 from sale only.
questions.explanationJa: Year 4の損益への影響：(1)旧トレーディング証券売却益：$22,000−$19,000＝$3,000の実現利益。(2)AFS→トレーディング振替：$4,000のAOCI未実現益は振替時に損益へ認識されますが（ASC 320-10-35-10）、設問の選択肢上は$3,000の純利益影響となります。

questions.stem: Kale Co. purchased bonds at a discount on the open market as an investment and intends to hold these bonds to maturity. Kale should account for these bonds at

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cost.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Amortized cost.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Fair value.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lower of cost or fair value.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 320, held-to-maturity (HTM) debt securities are measured at amortized cost, not fair value. Because Kale purchased the bonds at a discount, the discount must be amortized using the effective interest method over the life of the bonds, increasing the carrying value toward face value at maturity.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 320に従い、満期保有目的（HTM）の債務証券は償却原価で測定します。割引発行の場合、実効金利法で割引額を償却し、満期までに額面価値へ近づけます。

questions.stem: Puff Co. acquired 40% of Straw, Inc.'s voting common stock on January 2, Year 1, for $400,000. The carrying amount of Straw's net assets at the purchase date totaled $900,000. Fair values equaled carrying amounts for all items except equipment, for which fair values exceeded carrying amounts by $100,000. The equipment has a five-year life. Puff's policy is to amortize goodwill over ten years. During Year 1, Straw reported net income of $150,000. What amount of income from this investment should Puff report in its Year 1 income statement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 323, equity-method income = investor's share of investee net income minus amortization of purchase price differentials. Step 1: Puff's share of Straw's income = 40% × $150,000 = $60,000. Step 2: Fair value of Straw's net assets = $900,000 (book) + $100,000 (equipment excess) = $1,000,000. Puff paid $400,000 for 40%, implying 40% of $1,000,000 = $400,000 — no goodwill. Step 3: Amortization of equipment excess = 40% × ($100,000 / 5 years) = $8,000. Step 4: Under current GAAP, goodwill under equity method is not amortized (ASC 323-10-35-13). Income = $60,000 − $8,000 = $52,000. Equipment excess amortization = 40% × ($100,000 ÷ 5) = $8,000. Income = $60,000 − $8,000 = $52,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323に従い、持分法利益＝持分割合×被投資会社純利益−購入価格差額の償却。Puffの持分利益：40%×$150,000＝$60,000。設備超過額の償却：40%×($100,000÷5年)＝$8,000。のれん＝$0（識別可能純資産の公正価値40%＝$400,000＝取得原価）。持分法利益：$60,000−$8,000＝$52,000。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, Point, Inc., purchased 10% of Iona Co.'s common stock. Point purchased additional shares, bringing its ownership up to 40% of Iona's common stock outstanding on August 1, Year 3. During October Year 3, Iona declared and paid a cash dividend on all of its outstanding common stock. Point uses the equity method to account for its investment in Iona. How much income from the Iona investment should Point's Year 3 income statement report?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 10% of Iona's income for January 1 to July 31, Year 3, plus 40% of Iona's income for August 1 to December 31, Year 3.

choices.label: B
choices.text: 40% of Iona's income for August 1 to December 31, Year 3 only.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 40% of Iona's Year 3 income.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amount equal to dividends received from Iona.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323, when an investor acquires significant influence mid-year, the equity method is applied prospectively from the date significant influence was obtained. For the period before significant influence (January 1 – July 31), Point reports its proportionate share of income based on its then-10% ownership. After acquiring significant influence (August 1 – December 31), Point applies the equity method at 40%. Dividends received during the cost-method period reduce the investment carrying amount; they are not recognized as income under the equity method.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323に従い、重要な影響力を取得した時点から持分法を適用します。1/1〜7/31は10%所有のため費用法適用、持分利益はありません（配当が収益）。8/1〜12/31は40%で持分法を適用します。よって、1〜7月は10%×利益、8〜12月は40%×利益の合計が持分法利益となります。

questions.stem: Sun Corp. had investments in marketable debt securities costing $650,000 that were classified as available-for-sale. On June 30, Year 3, Sun decided to hold the investments to maturity and accordingly reclassified them to the held-to-maturity category on that date. The investments' market value was $575,000 at December 31, Year 2, $530,000 at June 30, Year 3, and $490,000 at December 31, Year 3. The changes in value were all determined to be noncredit related. Sun does not elect the fair value option to account for these investments. What amount should Sun report as net unrealized loss on marketable debt securities in its Year 3 statement of stockholders' equity?
questions.explanation: When AFS securities are reclassified to HTM (ASC 320-10-35-14), the unrealized loss at the transfer date is frozen in AOCI and amortized over the remaining life of the security. At June 30, Year 3 (transfer date), the unrealized loss = $530,000 fair value − $650,000 cost = −$120,000. This $120,000 unrealized loss remains in AOCI and is reported in the statement of stockholders' equity. After reclassification, subsequent fair value changes ($530,000 to $490,000 = −$40,000) are no longer recognized in AOCI because HTM is carried at amortized cost.
questions.explanationJa: AFS→HTM再分類時（ASC 320-10-35-14）、振替日の未実現損失はAOCIに据え置かれ残存期間で償却されます。振替日（6/30/Y3）の未実現損失：$530,000−$650,000＝−$120,000がAOCIに残ります。再分類後の公正価値変動（$530,000→$490,000）はHTMとして償却原価評価のため認識しません。

questions.stem: On July 1, in an exchange with commercial substance, Balt Co. exchanged a truck for 25 shares of Ace Corp ' s common stock. On that date, the truck ' s carrying amount was $2,500, and its fair value was $3,000. Also, the book value of Ace ' s stock was $60 per share. On December 31, Ace had 250 shares of common stock outstanding and its book value per share was $50. What amount should Balt report in its December 31 balance sheet as investment in Ace?
questions.explanation: In a nonmonetary exchange with commercial substance, the asset received is recorded at the fair value of the asset given up (or received, if more clearly evident). The truck's fair value on July 1 was $3,000, so the investment in Ace is initially recorded at $3,000. Balt holds 25 of 250 shares (10%), which is below the 20% threshold for significant influence, so the equity method does not apply. Because no readily determinable fair value for Ace's shares is given at December 31, the investment remains on the books at its initial cost of $3,000 (ASC 845-10, ASC 321-10).
questions.explanationJa: 経済的実質を伴う非貨幣資産の交換では、取得した資産は引き渡した資産の公正価値で計上します。トラックの公正価値は$3,000だったため、Ace株への投資は$3,000で記録されます。Baltは25/250=10%保有で重要な影響力の閾値（20%）未満のため持分法は適用されません。12月31日時点でAce株の容易に把握できる公正価値が示されていないため、投資は取得原価$3,000のまま計上されます（ASC 845-10、ASC 321-10）。
